}\\n866: \\n867: private:\\n868:   std::unique_ptr\\u003cPassConceptT\\u003e Pass;\\n869:   bool EagerlyInvalidate;\\n870: };\\n871: \\n872: /// A function to deduce a function pass type and wrap it in the\\n873: /// templated adaptor.\\n874: template \\u003ctypename FunctionPassT\\u003e\\n875: ModuleToFunctionPassAdaptor\\n876: createModuleToFunctionPassAdaptor(FunctionPassT \\u0026\\u0026Pass,\\n877:                                   bool EagerlyInvalidate = false) {\\n878:   using PassModelT =\\n879:       detail::PassModel\\u003cFunction, FunctionPassT, FunctionAnalysisManager\\u003e;\\n880:   // Do not use make_unique, it causes too many template instantiations,\\n881:   // causing terrible compile times.\\n882:   return ModuleToFunctionPassAdaptor(\\n883:       std::unique_ptr\\u003cModuleToFunctionPassAdaptor::PassConceptT\\u003e(\\n884:           new PassModelT(std::forward\\u003cFunctionPassT\\u003e(Pass))),\\n885:       EagerlyInvalidate);\\n886: }\\n887: \\n888: /// A utility pass template to force an analysis result to be available.\\n889: ///\\n890: /// If there are extra arguments at the pass's run level there may also be\\n891: /// extra arguments to the analysis manager's \\\\c getResult routine. We can't\\n892: /// guess how to effectively map the arguments from one to the other, and so\\n893: /// this specialization just ignores them.\\n894: ///\\n895: /// Specific patterns of run-method extra arguments and analysis manager extra\\n896: /// arguments will have to be defined as appropriate specializations.\\n897: template \\u003ctypename AnalysisT, typename IRUnitT,\\n898:           typename AnalysisManagerT = AnalysisManager\\u003cIRUnitT\\u003e,\\n899:           typename... ExtraArgTs\\u003e\\n900: struct RequireAnalysisPass\\n901:     : PassInfoMixin\\u003cRequireAnalysisPass\\u003cAnalysisT, IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT,\\n902:                                         ExtraArgTs...\\u003e\\u003e {\\n903:   /// Run this pass over some unit of IR.\\n904:   ///\\n905:   /// This pass can be run over any unit of IR and use any analysis manager\\n906:   /// provided they satisfy the basic API requirements. When this pass is\\n907:   /// created, these methods can be instantiated to satisfy whatever the\\n908:   /// context requires.\\n909:   PreservedAnalyses run(IRUnitT \\u0026Arg, AnalysisManagerT \\u0026AM,\\n910:                         ExtraArgTs \\u0026\\u0026... Args) {\\n911:     (void)AM.template getResult\\u003cAnalysisT\\u003e(Arg,\\n912:                                            std::forward\\u003cExtraArgTs\\u003e(Args)...);\\n913: \\n914:     return PreservedAnalyses::all();\\n915:   }\\n916:   void printPipeline(raw_ostream \\u0026OS,\\n917:                      function_ref\\u003cStringRef(StringRef)\\u003e MapClassName2PassName) {\\n918:     auto ClassName = AnalysisT::name();\\n919:     auto PassName = MapClassName2PassName(ClassName);\\n920:     OS \\u003c\\u003c \\\"require\\u003c\\\" \\u003c\\u003c PassName \\u003c\\u003c '\\u003e';\\n921:   }\\n922:   static bool isRequired() { return true; }\\n923: };\\n924: \\n925: /// A no-op pass template which simply forces a specific analysis result\\n926: /// to be invalidated.\\n927: template \\u003ctypename AnalysisT\\u003e\\n928: struct InvalidateAnalysisPass\\n929:     : PassInfoMixin\\u003cInvalidateAnalysisPass\\u003cAnalysisT\\u003e\\u003e {\\n930:   /// Run this pass over some unit of IR.\\n931:   ///\\n932:   /// This pass can be run over any unit of IR and use any analysis manager,\\n933:   /// provided they satisfy the basic API requirements. When this pass is\\n934:   /// created, these methods can be instantiated to satisfy whatever the\\n935:   /// context requires.\\n936:   template \\u003ctypename IRUnitT, typename AnalysisManagerT, typename... ExtraArgTs\\u003e\\n937:   PreservedAnalyses run(IRUnitT \\u0026Arg, AnalysisManagerT \\u0026AM, ExtraArgTs \\u0026\\u0026...) {\\n938:     auto PA = PreservedAnalyses::all();\\n939:     PA.abandon\\u003cAnalysisT\\u003e();\\n940:     return PA;\\n941:   }\\n942:   void printPipeline(raw_ostream \\u0026OS,\\n943:                      function_ref\\u003cStringRef(StringRef)\\u003e MapClassName2PassName) {\\n944:     auto ClassName = AnalysisT::name();\\n945:     auto PassName = MapClassName2PassName(ClassName);\\n946:     OS \\u003c\\u003c \\\"invalidate\\u003c\\\" \\u003c\\u003c PassName \\u003c\\u003c '\\u003e';\\n947:   }\\n948: };\\n949: \\n950: /// A utility pass that does nothing, but preserves no analyses.\\n951: ///\\n952: /// Because this preserves no analyses, any analysis passes queried after this\\n953: /// pass runs will recompute fresh results.\\n954: struct InvalidateAllAnalysesPass : PassInfoMixin\\u003cInvalidateAllAnalysesPass\\u003e {\\n955:   /// Run this pass over some unit of IR.\\n956:   template \\u003ctypename IRUnitT, typename AnalysisManagerT, typename... ExtraArgTs\\u003e\\n957:   PreservedAnalyses run(IRUnitT \\u0026, AnalysisManagerT \\u0026, ExtraArgTs \\u0026\\u0026...) {\\n958:     return PreservedAnalyses::none();\\n959:   }\\n960: };\\n961: \\n962: } // end namespace llvm\\n963: \\n964: #endif // LLVM_IR_PASSMANAGER_H\\n965: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h\"}", "annotations": null, "meta": null}], "structuredContent": {"content": "1: //===- PassManager.h - Pass management infrastructure -----------*- C++ -*-===//\n2: //\n3: // Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.\n4: // See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.\n5: // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception\n6: //\n7: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n8: /// \\file\n9: ///\n10: /// This header defines various interfaces for pass management in LLVM. There\n11: /// is no \"pass\" interface in LLVM per se. Instead, an instance of any class\n12: /// which supports a method to 'run' it over a unit of IR can be used as\n13: /// a pass. A pass manager is generally a tool to collect a sequence of passes\n14: /// which run over a particular IR construct, and run each of them in sequence\n15: /// over each such construct in the containing IR construct. As there is no\n16: /// containing IR construct for a Module, a manager for passes over modules\n17: /// forms the base case which runs its managed passes in sequence over the\n18: /// single module provided.\n19: ///\n20: /// The core IR library provides managers for running passes over\n21: /// modules and functions.\n22: ///\n23: /// * FunctionPassManager can run over a Module, runs each pass over\n24: ///   a Function.\n25: /// * ModulePassManager must be directly run, runs each pass over the Module.\n26: ///\n27: /// Note that the implementations of the pass managers use concept-based\n28: /// polymorphism as outlined in the \"Value Semantics and Concept-based\n29: /// Polymorphism\" talk (or its abbreviated sibling \"Inheritance Is The Base\n30: /// Class of Evil\") by Sean Parent:\n31: /// * https://sean-parent.stlab.cc/papers-and-presentations\n32: /// * http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_BpMYeUFXv8\n33: /// * https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/shows/goingnative-2013/inheritance-base-class-of-evil\n34: ///\n35: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n36: \n37: #ifndef LLVM_IR_PASSMANAGER_H\n38: #define LLVM_IR_PASSMANAGER_H\n39: \n40: #include \"llvm/ADT/DenseMap.h\"\n41: #include \"llvm/ADT/STLExtras.h\"\n42: #include \"llvm/ADT/StringExtras.h\"\n43: #include \"llvm/ADT/StringRef.h\"\n44: #include \"llvm/ADT/TinyPtrVector.h\"\n45: #include \"llvm/IR/Analysis.h\"\n46: #include \"llvm/IR/PassManagerInternal.h\"\n47: #include \"llvm/Support/Compiler.h\"\n48: #include \"llvm/Support/TypeName.h\"\n49: #include <cassert>\n50: #include <cstring>\n51: #include <list>\n52: #include <memory>\n53: #include <tuple>\n54: #include <type_traits>\n55: #include <utility>\n56: #include <vector>\n57: \n58: namespace llvm {\n59: \n60: class Function;\n61: class Module;\n62: \n63: // Forward declare the analysis manager template.\n64: template <typename IRUnitT, typename... ExtraArgTs> class AnalysisManager;\n65: \n66: /// A CRTP mix-in to automatically provide informational APIs needed for\n67: /// passes.\n68: ///\n69: /// This provides some boilerplate for types that are passes.\n70: template <typename DerivedT> struct PassInfoMixin {\n71:   /// Gets the name of the pass we are mixed into.\n72:   static StringRef name() {\n73:     static_assert(std::is_base_of<PassInfoMixin, DerivedT>::value,\n74:                   \"Must pass the derived type as the template argument!\");\n75:     StringRef Name = getTypeName<DerivedT>();\n76:     Name.consume_front(\"llvm::\");\n77:     return Name;\n78:   }\n79: \n80:   void printPipeline(raw_ostream &OS,\n81:                      function_ref<StringRef(StringRef)> MapClassName2PassName) {\n82:     StringRef ClassName = DerivedT::name();\n83:     auto PassName = MapClassName2PassName(ClassName);\n84:     OS << PassName;\n85:   }\n86: };\n87: \n88: /// A CRTP mix-in that provides informational APIs needed for analysis passes.\n89: ///\n90: /// This provides some boilerplate for types that are analysis passes. It\n91: /// automatically mixes in \\c PassInfoMixin.\n92: template <typename DerivedT>\n93: struct AnalysisInfoMixin : PassInfoMixin<DerivedT> {\n94:   /// Returns an opaque, unique ID for this analysis type.\n95:   ///\n96:   /// This ID is a pointer type that is guaranteed to be 8-byte aligned and thus\n97:   /// suitable for use in sets, maps, and other data structures that use the low\n98:   /// bits of pointers.\n99:   ///\n100:   /// Note that this requires the derived type provide a static \\c AnalysisKey\n101:   /// member called \\c Key.\n102:   ///\n103:   /// FIXME: The only reason the mixin type itself can't declare the Key value\n104:   /// is that some compilers cannot correctly unique a templated static variable\n105:   /// so it has the same addresses in each instantiation. The only currently\n106:   /// known platform with this limitation is Windows DLL builds, specifically\n107:   /// building each part of LLVM as a DLL. If we ever remove that build\n108:   /// configuration, this mixin can provide the static key as well.\n109:   static AnalysisKey *ID() {\n110:     static_assert(std::is_base_of<AnalysisInfoMixin, DerivedT>::value,\n111:                   \"Must pass the derived type as the template argument!\");\n112:     return &DerivedT::Key;\n113:   }\n114: };\n115: \n116: namespace detail {\n117: \n118: /// Actual unpacker of extra arguments in getAnalysisResult,\n119: /// passes only those tuple arguments that are mentioned in index_sequence.\n120: template <typename PassT, typename IRUnitT, typename AnalysisManagerT,\n121:           typename... ArgTs, size_t... Ns>\n122: typename PassT::Result\n123: getAnalysisResultUnpackTuple(AnalysisManagerT &AM, IRUnitT &IR,\n124:                              std::tuple<ArgTs...> Args,\n125:                              std::index_sequence<Ns...>) {\n126:   (void)Args;\n127:   return AM.template getResult<PassT>(IR, std::get<Ns>(Args)...);\n128: }\n129: \n130: /// Helper for *partial* unpacking of extra arguments in getAnalysisResult.\n131: ///\n132: /// Arguments passed in tuple come from PassManager, so they might have extra\n133: /// arguments after those AnalysisManager's ExtraArgTs ones that we need to\n134: /// pass to getResult.\n135: template <typename PassT, typename IRUnitT, typename... AnalysisArgTs,\n136:           typename... MainArgTs>\n137: typename PassT::Result\n138: getAnalysisResult(AnalysisManager<IRUnitT, AnalysisArgTs...> &AM, IRUnitT &IR,\n139:                   std::tuple<MainArgTs...> Args) {\n140:   return (getAnalysisResultUnpackTuple<\n141:           PassT, IRUnitT>)(AM, IR, Args,\n142:                            std::index_sequence_for<AnalysisArgTs...>{});\n143: }\n144: \n145: } // namespace detail\n146: \n147: /// Manages a sequence of passes over a particular unit of IR.\n148: ///\n149: /// A pass manager contains a sequence of passes to run over a particular unit\n150: /// of IR (e.g. Functions, Modules). It is itself a valid pass over that unit of\n151: /// IR, and when run over some given IR will run each of its contained passes in\n152: /// sequence. Pass managers are the primary and most basic building block of a\n153: /// pass pipeline.\n154: ///\n155: /// When you run a pass manager, you provide an \\c AnalysisManager<IRUnitT>\n156: /// argument. The pass manager will propagate that analysis manager to each\n157: /// pass it runs, and will call the analysis manager's invalidation routine with\n158: /// the PreservedAnalyses of each pass it runs.\n159: template <typename IRUnitT,\n160:           typename AnalysisManagerT = AnalysisManager<IRUnitT>,\n161:           typename... ExtraArgTs>\n162: class PassManager : public PassInfoMixin<\n163:                         PassManager<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>> {\n164: public:\n165:   /// Construct a pass manager.\n166:   explicit PassManager() = default;\n167: \n168:   // FIXME: These are equivalent to the default move constructor/move\n169:   // assignment. However, using = default triggers linker errors due to the\n170:   // explicit instantiations below. Find away to use the default and remove the\n171:   // duplicated code here.\n172:   PassManager(PassManager &&Arg) : Passes(std::move(Arg.Passes)) {}\n173: \n174:   PassManager &operator=(PassManager &&RHS) {\n175:     Passes = std::move(RHS.Passes);\n176:     return *this;\n177:   }\n178: \n179:   void printPipeline(raw_ostream &OS,\n180:                      function_ref<StringRef(StringRef)> MapClassName2PassName) {\n181:     ListSeparator LS(\",\");\n182:     for (auto &P : Passes) {\n183:       OS << LS;\n184:       P->printPipeline(OS, MapClassName2PassName);\n185:     }\n186:   }\n187: \n188:   /// Run all of the passes in this manager over the given unit of IR.\n189:   /// ExtraArgs are passed to each pass.\n190:   PreservedAnalyses run(IRUnitT &IR, AnalysisManagerT &AM,\n191:                         ExtraArgTs... ExtraArgs);\n192: \n193:   template <typename PassT>\n194:   LLVM_ATTRIBUTE_MINSIZE std::enable_if_t<!std::is_same_v<PassT, PassManager>>\n195:   addPass(PassT &&Pass) {\n196:     using PassModelT =\n197:         detail::PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>;\n198:     // Do not use make_unique or emplace_back, they cause too many template\n199:     // instantiations, causing terrible compile times.\n200:     Passes.push_back(std::unique_ptr<PassConceptT>(\n201:         new PassModelT(std::forward<PassT>(Pass))));\n202:   }\n203: \n204:   /// When adding a pass manager pass that has the same type as this pass\n205:   /// manager, simply move the passes over. This is because we don't have\n206:   /// use cases rely on executing nested pass managers. Doing this could\n207:   /// reduce implementation complexity and avoid potential invalidation\n208:   /// issues that may happen with nested pass managers of the same type.\n209:   template <typename PassT>\n210:   LLVM_ATTRIBUTE_MINSIZE std::enable_if_t<std::is_same_v<PassT, PassManager>>\n211:   addPass(PassT &&Pass) {\n212:     for (auto &P : Pass.Passes)\n213:       Passes.push_back(std::move(P));\n214:   }\n215: \n216:   /// Returns if the pass manager contains any passes.\n217:   bool isEmpty() const { return Passes.empty(); }\n218: \n219:   static bool isRequired() { return true; }\n220: \n221: protected:\n222:   using PassConceptT =\n223:       detail::PassConcept<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>;\n224: \n225:   std::vector<std::unique_ptr<PassConceptT>> Passes;\n226: };\n227: \n228: template <typename IRUnitT>\n229: void printIRUnitNameForStackTrace(raw_ostream &OS, const IRUnitT &IR);\n230: \n231: template <>\n232: LLVM_ABI void printIRUnitNameForStackTrace<Module>(raw_ostream &OS,\n233:                                                    const Module &IR);\n234: \n235: extern template class LLVM_TEMPLATE_ABI PassManager<Module>;\n236: \n237: /// Convenience typedef for a pass manager over modules.\n238: using ModulePassManager = PassManager<Module>;\n239: \n240: template <>\n241: LLVM_ABI void printIRUnitNameForStackTrace<Function>(raw_ostream &OS,\n242:                                                      const Function &IR);\n243: \n244: extern template class LLVM_TEMPLATE_ABI PassManager<Function>;\n245: \n246: /// Convenience typedef for a pass manager over functions.\n247: using FunctionPassManager = PassManager<Function>;\n248: \n249: /// A container for analyses that lazily runs them and caches their\n250: /// results.\n251: ///\n252: /// This class can manage analyses for any IR unit where the address of the IR\n253: /// unit sufficies as its identity.\n254: template <typename IRUnitT, typename... ExtraArgTs> class AnalysisManager {\n255: public:\n256:   class Invalidator;\n257: \n258: private:\n259:   // Now that we've defined our invalidator, we can define the concept types.\n260:   using ResultConceptT = detail::AnalysisResultConcept<IRUnitT, Invalidator>;\n261:   using PassConceptT =\n262:       detail::AnalysisPassConcept<IRUnitT, Invalidator, ExtraArgTs...>;\n263: \n264:   /// List of analysis pass IDs and associated concept pointers.\n265:   ///\n266:   /// Requires iterators to be valid across appending new entries and arbitrary\n267:   /// erases. Provides the analysis ID to enable finding iterators to a given\n268:   /// entry in maps below, and provides the storage for the actual result\n269:   /// concept.\n270:   using AnalysisResultListT =\n271:       std::list<std::pair<AnalysisKey *, std::unique_ptr<ResultConceptT>>>;\n272: \n273:   /// Map type from IRUnitT pointer to our custom list type.\n274:   using AnalysisResultListMapT = DenseMap<IRUnitT *, AnalysisResultListT>;\n275: \n276:   /// Map type from a pair of analysis ID and IRUnitT pointer to an\n277:   /// iterator into a particular result list (which is where the actual analysis\n278:   /// result is stored).\n279:   using AnalysisResultMapT =\n280:       DenseMap<std::pair<AnalysisKey *, IRUnitT *>,\n281:                typename AnalysisResultListT::iterator>;\n282: \n283: public:\n284:   /// API to communicate dependencies between analyses during invalidation.\n285:   ///\n286:   /// When an analysis result embeds handles to other analysis results, it\n287:   /// needs to be invalidated both when its own information isn't preserved and\n288:   /// when any of its embedded analysis results end up invalidated. We pass an\n289:   /// \\c Invalidator object as an argument to \\c invalidate() in order to let\n290:   /// the analysis results themselves define the dependency graph on the fly.\n291:   /// This lets us avoid building an explicit representation of the\n292:   /// dependencies between analysis results.\n293:   class Invalidator {\n294:   public:\n295:     /// Trigger the invalidation of some other analysis pass if not already\n296:     /// handled and return whether it was in fact invalidated.\n297:     ///\n298:     /// This is expected to be called from within a given analysis result's \\c\n299:     /// invalidate method to trigger a depth-first walk of all inter-analysis\n300:     /// dependencies. The same \\p IR unit and \\p PA passed to that result's \\c\n301:     /// invalidate method should in turn be provided to this routine.\n302:     ///\n303:     /// The first time this is called for a given analysis pass, it will call\n304:     /// the corresponding result's \\c invalidate method.  Subsequent calls will\n305:     /// use a cache of the results of that initial call.  It is an error to form\n306:     /// cyclic dependencies between analysis results.\n307:     ///\n308:     /// This returns true if the given analysis's result is invalid. Any\n309:     /// dependecies on it will become invalid as a result.\n310:     template <typename PassT>\n311:     bool invalidate(IRUnitT &IR, const PreservedAnalyses &PA) {\n312:       using ResultModelT =\n313:           detail::AnalysisResultModel<IRUnitT, PassT, typename PassT::Result,\n314:                                       Invalidator>;\n315: \n316:       return invalidateImpl<ResultModelT>(PassT::ID(), IR, PA);\n317:     }\n318: \n319:     /// A type-erased variant of the above invalidate method with the same core\n320:     /// API other than passing an analysis ID rather than an analysis type\n321:     /// parameter.\n322:     ///\n323:     /// This is sadly less efficient than the above routine, which leverages\n324:     /// the type parameter to avoid the type erasure overhead.\n325:     bool invalidate(AnalysisKey *ID, IRUnitT &IR, const PreservedAnalyses &PA) {\n326:       return invalidateImpl<>(ID, IR, PA);\n327:     }\n328: \n329:   private:\n330:     friend class AnalysisManager;\n331: \n332:     template <typename ResultT = ResultConceptT>\n333:     bool invalidateImpl(AnalysisKey *ID, IRUnitT &IR,\n334:                         const PreservedAnalyses &PA) {\n335:       // If we've already visited this pass, return true if it was invalidated\n336:       // and false otherwise.\n337:       auto IMapI = IsResultInvalidated.find(ID);\n338:       if (IMapI != IsResultInvalidated.end())\n339:         return IMapI->second;\n340: \n341:       // Otherwise look up the result object.\n342:       auto RI = Results.find({ID, &IR});\n343:       assert(RI != Results.end() &&\n344:              \"Trying to invalidate a dependent result that isn't in the \"\n345:              \"manager's cache is always an error, likely due to a stale result \"\n346:              \"handle!\");\n347: \n348:       auto &Result = static_cast<ResultT &>(*RI->second->second);\n349: \n350:       // Insert into the map whether the result should be invalidated and return\n351:       // that. Note that we cannot reuse IMapI and must do a fresh insert here,\n352:       // as calling invalidate could (recursively) insert things into the map,\n353:       // making any iterator or reference invalid.\n354:       bool Inserted;\n355:       std::tie(IMapI, Inserted) =\n356:           IsResultInvalidated.insert({ID, Result.invalidate(IR, PA, *this)});\n357:       (void)Inserted;\n358:       assert(Inserted && \"Should not have already inserted this ID, likely \"\n359:                          \"indicates a dependency cycle!\");\n360:       return IMapI->second;\n361:     }\n362: \n363:     Invalidator(SmallDenseMap<AnalysisKey *, bool, 8> &IsResultInvalidated,\n364:                 const AnalysisResultMapT &Results)\n365:         : IsResultInvalidated(IsResultInvalidated), Results(Results) {}\n366: \n367:     SmallDenseMap<AnalysisKey *, bool, 8> &IsResultInvalidated;\n368:     const AnalysisResultMapT &Results;\n369:   };\n370: \n371:   /// Construct an empty analysis manager.\n372:   AnalysisManager();\n373:   AnalysisManager(AnalysisManager &&);\n374:   AnalysisManager &operator=(AnalysisManager &&);\n375: \n376:   /// Returns true if the analysis manager has an empty results cache.\n377:   bool empty() const {\n378:     assert(AnalysisResults.empty() == AnalysisResultLists.empty() &&\n379:            \"The storage and index of analysis results disagree on how many \"\n380:            \"there are!\");\n381:     return AnalysisResults.empty();\n382:   }\n383: \n384:   /// Clear any cached analysis results for a single unit of IR.\n385:   ///\n386:   /// This doesn't invalidate, but instead simply deletes, the relevant results.\n387:   /// It is useful when the IR is being removed and we want to clear out all the\n388:   /// memory pinned for it.\n389:   void clear(IRUnitT &IR, llvm::StringRef Name);\n390: \n391:   /// Clear all analysis results cached by this AnalysisManager.\n392:   ///\n393:   /// Like \\c clear(IRUnitT&), this doesn't invalidate the results; it simply\n394:   /// deletes them.  This lets you clean up the AnalysisManager when the set of\n395:   /// IR units itself has potentially changed, and thus we can't even look up a\n396:   /// a result and invalidate/clear it directly.\n397:   void clear() {\n398:     AnalysisResults.clear();\n399:     AnalysisResultLists.clear();\n400:   }\n401: \n402:   /// Returns true if the specified analysis pass is registered.\n403:   template <typename PassT> bool isPassRegistered() const {\n404:     return AnalysisPasses.count(PassT::ID());\n405:   }\n406: \n407:   /// Get the result of an analysis pass for a given IR unit.\n408:   ///\n409:   /// Runs the analysis if a cached result is not available.\n410:   template <typename PassT>\n411:   typename PassT::Result &getResult(IRUnitT &IR, ExtraArgTs... ExtraArgs) {\n412:     assert(AnalysisPasses.count(PassT::ID()) &&\n413:            \"This analysis pass was not registered prior to being queried\");\n414:     ResultConceptT &ResultConcept =\n415:         getResultImpl(PassT::ID(), IR, ExtraArgs...);\n416: \n417:     using ResultModelT =\n418:         detail::AnalysisResultModel<IRUnitT, PassT, typename PassT::Result,\n419:                                     Invalidator>;\n420: \n421:     return static_cast<ResultModelT &>(ResultConcept).Result;\n422:   }\n423: \n424:   /// Get the cached result of an analysis pass for a given IR unit.\n425:   ///\n426:   /// This method never runs the analysis.\n427:   ///\n428:   /// \\returns null if there is no cached result.\n429:   template <typename PassT>\n430:   typename PassT::Result *getCachedResult(IRUnitT &IR) const {\n431:     assert(AnalysisPasses.count(PassT::ID()) &&\n432:            \"This analysis pass was not registered prior to being queried\");\n433: \n434:     ResultConceptT *ResultConcept = getCachedResultImpl(PassT::ID(), IR);\n435:     if (!ResultConcept)\n436:       return nullptr;\n437: \n438:     using ResultModelT =\n439:         detail::AnalysisResultModel<IRUnitT, PassT, typename PassT::Result,\n440:                                     Invalidator>;\n441: \n442:     return &static_cast<ResultModelT *>(ResultConcept)->Result;\n443:   }\n444: \n445:   /// Verify that the given Result cannot be invalidated, assert otherwise.\n446:   template <typename PassT>\n447:   void verifyNotInvalidated(IRUnitT &IR, typename PassT::Result *Result) const {\n448:     PreservedAnalyses PA = PreservedAnalyses::none();\n449:     SmallDenseMap<AnalysisKey *, bool, 8> IsResultInvalidated;\n450:     Invalidator Inv(IsResultInvalidated, AnalysisResults);\n451:     assert(!Result->invalidate(IR, PA, Inv) &&\n452:            \"Cached result cannot be invalidated\");\n453:   }\n454: \n455:   /// Register an analysis pass with the manager.\n456:   ///\n457:   /// The parameter is a callable whose result is an analysis pass. This allows\n458:   /// passing in a lambda to construct the analysis.\n459:   ///\n460:   /// The analysis type to register is the type returned by calling the \\c\n461:   /// PassBuilder argument. If that type has already been registered, then the\n462:   /// argument will not be called and this function will return false.\n463:   /// Otherwise, we register the analysis returned by calling \\c PassBuilder(),\n464:   /// and this function returns true.\n465:   ///\n466:   /// (Note: Although the return value of this function indicates whether or not\n467:   /// an analysis was previously registered, you should just register all the\n468:   /// analyses you might want and let this class run them lazily.  This idiom\n469:   /// lets us minimize the number of times we have to look up analyses in our\n470:   /// hashtable.)\n471:   template <typename PassBuilderT>\n472:   bool registerPass(PassBuilderT &&PassBuilder) {\n473:     using PassT = decltype(PassBuilder());\n474:     using PassModelT =\n475:         detail::AnalysisPassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, Invalidator, ExtraArgTs...>;\n476: \n477:     auto &PassPtr = AnalysisPasses[PassT::ID()];\n478:     if (PassPtr)\n479:       // Already registered this pass type!\n480:       return false;\n481: \n482:     // Construct a new model around the instance returned by the builder.\n483:     PassPtr.reset(new PassModelT(PassBuilder()));\n484:     return true;\n485:   }\n486: \n487:   /// Invalidate cached analyses for an IR unit.\n488:   ///\n489:   /// Walk through all of the analyses pertaining to this unit of IR and\n490:   /// invalidate them, unless they are preserved by the PreservedAnalyses set.\n491:   void invalidate(IRUnitT &IR, const PreservedAnalyses &PA);\n492: \n493:   /// Directly clear a cached analysis for an IR unit.\n494:   ///\n495:   /// Using invalidate() over this is preferred unless you are really\n496:   /// sure you want to *only* clear this analysis without asking if it is\n497:   /// invalid.\n498:   template <typename AnalysisT> void clearAnalysis(IRUnitT &IR) {\n499:     AnalysisResultListT &ResultsList = AnalysisResultLists[&IR];\n500:     AnalysisKey *ID = AnalysisT::ID();\n501: \n502:     auto I =\n503:         llvm::find_if(ResultsList, [&ID](auto &E) { return E.first == ID; });\n504:     assert(I != ResultsList.end() && \"Analysis must be available\");\n505:     ResultsList.erase(I);\n506:     AnalysisResults.erase({ID, &IR});\n507:   }\n508: \n509: private:\n510:   /// Look up a registered analysis pass.\n511:   PassConceptT &lookUpPass(AnalysisKey *ID) {\n512:     typename AnalysisPassMapT::iterator PI = AnalysisPasses.find(ID);\n513:     assert(PI != AnalysisPasses.end() &&\n514:            \"Analysis passes must be registered prior to being queried!\");\n515:     return *PI->second;\n516:   }\n517: \n518:   /// Look up a registered analysis pass.\n519:   const PassConceptT &lookUpPass(AnalysisKey *ID) const {\n520:     typename AnalysisPassMapT::const_iterator PI = AnalysisPasses.find(ID);\n521:     assert(PI != AnalysisPasses.end() &&\n522:            \"Analysis passes must be registered prior to being queried!\");\n523:     return *PI->second;\n524:   }\n525: \n526:   /// Get an analysis result, running the pass if necessary.\n527:   ResultConceptT &getResultImpl(AnalysisKey *ID, IRUnitT &IR,\n528:                                 ExtraArgTs... ExtraArgs);\n529: \n530:   /// Get a cached analysis result or return null.\n531:   ResultConceptT *getCachedResultImpl(AnalysisKey *ID, IRUnitT &IR) const {\n532:     typename AnalysisResultMapT::const_iterator RI =\n533:         AnalysisResults.find({ID, &IR});\n534:     return RI == AnalysisResults.end() ? nullptr : &*RI->second->second;\n535:   }\n536: \n537:   /// Map type from analysis pass ID to pass concept pointer.\n538:   using AnalysisPassMapT =\n539:       DenseMap<AnalysisKey *, std::unique_ptr<PassConceptT>>;\n540: \n541:   /// Collection of analysis passes, indexed by ID.\n542:   AnalysisPassMapT AnalysisPasses;\n543: \n544:   /// Map from IR unit to a list of analysis results.\n545:   ///\n546:   /// Provides linear time removal of all analysis results for a IR unit and\n547:   /// the ultimate storage for a particular cached analysis result.\n548:   AnalysisResultListMapT AnalysisResultLists;\n549: \n550:   /// Map from an analysis ID and IR unit to a particular cached\n551:   /// analysis result.\n552:   AnalysisResultMapT AnalysisResults;\n553: };\n554: \n555: extern template class LLVM_TEMPLATE_ABI AnalysisManager<Module>;\n556: \n557: /// Convenience typedef for the Module analysis manager.\n558: using ModuleAnalysisManager = AnalysisManager<Module>;\n559: \n560: extern template class LLVM_TEMPLATE_ABI AnalysisManager<Function>;\n561: \n562: /// Convenience typedef for the Function analysis manager.\n563: using FunctionAnalysisManager = AnalysisManager<Function>;\n564: \n565: /// An analysis over an \"outer\" IR unit that provides access to an\n566: /// analysis manager over an \"inner\" IR unit.  The inner unit must be contained\n567: /// in the outer unit.\n568: ///\n569: /// For example, InnerAnalysisManagerProxy<FunctionAnalysisManager, Module> is\n570: /// an analysis over Modules (the \"outer\" unit) that provides access to a\n571: /// Function analysis manager.  The FunctionAnalysisManager is the \"inner\"\n572: /// manager being proxied, and Functions are the \"inner\" unit.  The inner/outer\n573: /// relationship is valid because each Function is contained in one Module.\n574: ///\n575: /// If you're (transitively) within a pass manager for an IR unit U that\n576: /// contains IR unit V, you should never use an analysis manager over V, except\n577: /// via one of these proxies.\n578: ///\n579: /// Note that the proxy's result is a move-only RAII object.  The validity of\n580: /// the analyses in the inner analysis manager is tied to its lifetime.\n581: template <typename AnalysisManagerT, typename IRUnitT, typename... ExtraArgTs>\n582: class LLVM_TEMPLATE_ABI InnerAnalysisManagerProxy\n583:     : public AnalysisInfoMixin<\n584:           InnerAnalysisManagerProxy<AnalysisManagerT, IRUnitT>> {\n585: public:\n586:   class Result {\n587:   public:\n588:     explicit Result(AnalysisManagerT &InnerAM) : InnerAM(&InnerAM) {}\n589: \n590:     Result(Result &&Arg) : InnerAM(std::move(Arg.InnerAM)) {\n591:       // We have to null out the analysis manager in the moved-from state\n592:       // because we are taking ownership of the responsibility to clear the\n593:       // analysis state.\n594:       Arg.InnerAM = nullptr;\n595:     }\n596: \n597:     ~Result() {\n598:       // InnerAM is cleared in a moved from state where there is nothing to do.\n599:       if (!InnerAM)\n600:         return;\n601: \n602:       // Clear out the analysis manager if we're being destroyed -- it means we\n603:       // didn't even see an invalidate call when we got invalidated.\n604:       InnerAM->clear();\n605:     }\n606: \n607:     Result &operator=(Result &&RHS) {\n608:       InnerAM = RHS.InnerAM;\n609:       // We have to null out the analysis manager in the moved-from state\n610:       // because we are taking ownership of the responsibility to clear the\n611:       // analysis state.\n612:       RHS.InnerAM = nullptr;\n613:       return *this;\n614:     }\n615: \n616:     /// Accessor for the analysis manager.\n617:     AnalysisManagerT &getManager() { return *InnerAM; }\n618: \n619:     /// Handler for invalidation of the outer IR unit, \\c IRUnitT.\n620:     ///\n621:     /// If the proxy analysis itself is not preserved, we assume that the set of\n622:     /// inner IR objects contained in IRUnit may have changed.  In this case,\n623:     /// we have to call \\c clear() on the inner analysis manager, as it may now\n624:     /// have stale pointers to its inner IR objects.\n625:     ///\n626:     /// Regardless of whether the proxy analysis is marked as preserved, all of\n627:     /// the analyses in the inner analysis manager are potentially invalidated\n628:     /// based on the set of preserved analyses.\n629:     bool invalidate(\n630:         IRUnitT &IR, const PreservedAnalyses &PA,\n631:         typename AnalysisManager<IRUnitT, ExtraArgTs...>::Invalidator &Inv);\n632: \n633:   private:\n634:     AnalysisManagerT *InnerAM;\n635:   };\n636: \n637:   explicit InnerAnalysisManagerProxy(AnalysisManagerT &InnerAM)\n638:       : InnerAM(&InnerAM) {}\n639: \n640:   /// Run the analysis pass and create our proxy result object.\n641:   ///\n642:   /// This doesn't do any interesting work; it is primarily used to insert our\n643:   /// proxy result object into the outer analysis cache so that we can proxy\n644:   /// invalidation to the inner analysis manager.\n645:   Result run(IRUnitT &IR, AnalysisManager<IRUnitT, ExtraArgTs...> &AM,\n646:              ExtraArgTs...) {\n647:     return Result(*InnerAM);\n648:   }\n649: \n650: private:\n651:   friend AnalysisInfoMixin<\n652:       InnerAnalysisManagerProxy<AnalysisManagerT, IRUnitT>>;\n653: \n654:   static AnalysisKey Key;\n655: \n656:   AnalysisManagerT *InnerAM;\n657: };\n658: \n659: // NOTE: The LLVM_ABI annotation cannot be used here because MSVC disallows\n660: // storage-class specifiers on class members outside of the class declaration\n661: // (C2720). LLVM_ATTRIBUTE_VISIBILITY_DEFAULT only applies to non-Windows\n662: // targets so it is used instead. Without this annotation, compiling LLVM as a\n663: // shared library with -fvisibility=hidden using GCC fails to export the symbol\n664: // even though InnerAnalysisManagerProxy is already annotated with LLVM_ABI.\n665: template <typename AnalysisManagerT, typename IRUnitT, typename... ExtraArgTs>\n666: LLVM_ATTRIBUTE_VISIBILITY_DEFAULT AnalysisKey\n667:     InnerAnalysisManagerProxy<AnalysisManagerT, IRUnitT, ExtraArgTs...>::Key;\n668: \n669: /// Provide the \\c FunctionAnalysisManager to \\c Module proxy.\n670: using FunctionAnalysisManagerModuleProxy =\n671:     InnerAnalysisManagerProxy<FunctionAnalysisManager, Module>;\n672: \n673: /// Specialization of the invalidate method for the \\c\n674: /// FunctionAnalysisManagerModuleProxy's result.\n675: template <>\n676: LLVM_ABI bool FunctionAnalysisManagerModuleProxy::Result::invalidate(\n677:     Module &M, const PreservedAnalyses &PA,\n678:     ModuleAnalysisManager::Invalidator &Inv);\n679: \n680: // Ensure the \\c FunctionAnalysisManagerModuleProxy is provided as an extern\n681: // template.\n682: extern template class InnerAnalysisManagerProxy<FunctionAnalysisManager,\n683:                                                 Module>;\n684: \n685: /// An analysis over an \"inner\" IR unit that provides access to an\n686: /// analysis manager over a \"outer\" IR unit.  The inner unit must be contained\n687: /// in the outer unit.\n688: ///\n689: /// For example OuterAnalysisManagerProxy<ModuleAnalysisManager, Function> is an\n690: /// analysis over Functions (the \"inner\" unit) which provides access to a Module\n691: /// analysis manager.  The ModuleAnalysisManager is the \"outer\" manager being\n692: /// proxied, and Modules are the \"outer\" IR unit.  The inner/outer relationship\n693: /// is valid because each Function is contained in one Module.\n694: ///\n695: /// This proxy only exposes the const interface of the outer analysis manager,\n696: /// to indicate that you cannot cause an outer analysis to run from within an\n697: /// inner pass.  Instead, you must rely on the \\c getCachedResult API.  This is\n698: /// due to keeping potential future concurrency in mind. To give an example,\n699: /// running a module analysis before any function passes may give a different\n700: /// result than running it in a function pass. Both may be valid, but it would\n701: /// produce non-deterministic results. GlobalsAA is a good analysis example,\n702: /// because the cached information has the mod/ref info for all memory for each\n703: /// function at the time the analysis was computed. The information is still\n704: /// valid after a function transformation, but it may be *different* if\n705: /// recomputed after that transform. GlobalsAA is never invalidated.\n706: \n707: ///\n708: /// This proxy doesn't manage invalidation in any way -- that is handled by the\n709: /// recursive return path of each layer of the pass manager.  A consequence of\n710: /// this is the outer analyses may be stale.  We invalidate the outer analyses\n711: /// only when we're done running passes over the inner IR units.\n712: template <typename AnalysisManagerT, typename IRUnitT, typename... ExtraArgTs>\n713: class OuterAnalysisManagerProxy\n714:     : public AnalysisInfoMixin<\n715:           OuterAnalysisManagerProxy<AnalysisManagerT, IRUnitT, ExtraArgTs...>> {\n716: public:\n717:   /// Result proxy object for \\c OuterAnalysisManagerProxy.\n718:   class Result {\n719:   public:\n720:     explicit Result(const AnalysisManagerT &OuterAM) : OuterAM(&OuterAM) {}\n721: \n722:     /// Get a cached analysis. If the analysis can be invalidated, this will\n723:     /// assert.\n724:     template <typename PassT, typename IRUnitTParam>\n725:     typename PassT::Result *getCachedResult(IRUnitTParam &IR) const {\n726:       typename PassT::Result *Res =\n727:           OuterAM->template getCachedResult<PassT>(IR);\n728:       if (Res)\n729:         OuterAM->template verifyNotInvalidated<PassT>(IR, Res);\n730:       return Res;\n731:     }\n732: \n733:     /// Method provided for unit testing, not intended for general use.\n734:     template <typename PassT, typename IRUnitTParam>\n735:     bool cachedResultExists(IRUnitTParam &IR) const {\n736:       typename PassT::Result *Res =\n737:           OuterAM->template getCachedResult<PassT>(IR);\n738:       return Res != nullptr;\n739:     }\n740: \n741:     /// When invalidation occurs, remove any registered invalidation events.\n742:     bool invalidate(\n743:         IRUnitT &IRUnit, const PreservedAnalyses &PA,\n744:         typename AnalysisManager<IRUnitT, ExtraArgTs...>::Invalidator &Inv) {\n745:       // Loop over the set of registered outer invalidation mappings and if any\n746:       // of them map to an analysis that is now invalid, clear it out.\n747:       SmallVector<AnalysisKey *, 4> DeadKeys;\n748:       for (auto &KeyValuePair : OuterAnalysisInvalidationMap) {\n749:         AnalysisKey *OuterID = KeyValuePair.first;\n750:         auto &InnerIDs = KeyValuePair.second;\n751:         llvm::erase_if(InnerIDs, [&](AnalysisKey *InnerID) {\n752:           return Inv.invalidate(InnerID, IRUnit, PA);\n753:         });\n754:         if (InnerIDs.empty())\n755:           DeadKeys.push_back(OuterID);\n756:       }\n757: \n758:       for (auto *OuterID : DeadKeys)\n759:         OuterAnalysisInvalidationMap.erase(OuterID);\n760: \n761:       // The proxy itself remains valid regardless of anything else.\n762:       return false;\n763:     }\n764: \n765:     /// Register a deferred invalidation event for when the outer analysis\n766:     /// manager processes its invalidations.\n767:     template <typename OuterAnalysisT, typename InvalidatedAnalysisT>\n768:     void registerOuterAnalysisInvalidation() {\n769:       AnalysisKey *OuterID = OuterAnalysisT::ID();\n770:       AnalysisKey *InvalidatedID = InvalidatedAnalysisT::ID();\n771: \n772:       auto &InvalidatedIDList = OuterAnalysisInvalidationMap[OuterID];\n773:       // Note, this is a linear scan. If we end up with large numbers of\n774:       // analyses that all trigger invalidation on the same outer analysis,\n775:       // this entire system should be changed to some other deterministic\n776:       // data structure such as a `SetVector` of a pair of pointers.\n777:       if (!llvm::is_contained(InvalidatedIDList, InvalidatedID))\n778:         InvalidatedIDList.push_back(InvalidatedID);\n779:     }\n780: \n781:     /// Access the map from outer analyses to deferred invalidation requiring\n782:     /// analyses.\n783:     const SmallDenseMap<AnalysisKey *, TinyPtrVector<AnalysisKey *>, 2> &\n784:     getOuterInvalidations() const {\n785:       return OuterAnalysisInvalidationMap;\n786:     }\n787: \n788:   private:\n789:     const AnalysisManagerT *OuterAM;\n790: \n791:     /// A map from an outer analysis ID to the set of this IR-unit's analyses\n792:     /// which need to be invalidated.\n793:     SmallDenseMap<AnalysisKey *, TinyPtrVector<AnalysisKey *>, 2>\n794:         OuterAnalysisInvalidationMap;\n795:   };\n796: \n797:   OuterAnalysisManagerProxy(const AnalysisManagerT &OuterAM)\n798:       : OuterAM(&OuterAM) {}\n799: \n800:   /// Run the analysis pass and create our proxy result object.\n801:   /// Nothing to see here, it just forwards the \\c OuterAM reference into the\n802:   /// result.\n803:   Result run(IRUnitT &, AnalysisManager<IRUnitT, ExtraArgTs...> &,\n804:              ExtraArgTs...) {\n805:     return Result(*OuterAM);\n806:   }\n807: \n808: private:\n809:   friend AnalysisInfoMixin<\n810:       OuterAnalysisManagerProxy<AnalysisManagerT, IRUnitT, ExtraArgTs...>>;\n811: \n812:   static AnalysisKey Key;\n813: \n814:   const AnalysisManagerT *OuterAM;\n815: };\n816: \n817: template <typename AnalysisManagerT, typename IRUnitT, typename... ExtraArgTs>\n818: AnalysisKey\n819:     OuterAnalysisManagerProxy<AnalysisManagerT, IRUnitT, ExtraArgTs...>::Key;\n820: \n821: extern template class LLVM_TEMPLATE_ABI\n822:     OuterAnalysisManagerProxy<ModuleAnalysisManager, Function>;\n823: /// Provide the \\c ModuleAnalysisManager to \\c Function proxy.\n824: using ModuleAnalysisManagerFunctionProxy =\n825:     OuterAnalysisManagerProxy<ModuleAnalysisManager, Function>;\n826: \n827: /// Trivial adaptor that maps from a module to its functions.\n828: ///\n829: /// Designed to allow composition of a FunctionPass(Manager) and\n830: /// a ModulePassManager, by running the FunctionPass(Manager) over every\n831: /// function in the module.\n832: ///\n833: /// Function passes run within this adaptor can rely on having exclusive access\n834: /// to the function they are run over. We can't\n892: /// guess how to effectively map the arguments from one to the other, and so\n893: /// this specialization just ignores them.\n894: ///\n895: /// Specific patterns of run-method extra arguments and analysis manager extra\n896: /// arguments will have to be defined as appropriate specializations.\n897: template <typename AnalysisT, typename IRUnitT,\n898:           typename AnalysisManagerT = AnalysisManager<IRUnitT>,\n899:           typename... ExtraArgTs>\n900: struct RequireAnalysisPass\n901:     : PassInfoMixin<RequireAnalysisPass<AnalysisT, IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT,\n902:                                         ExtraArgTs...>> {\n903:   /// Run this pass over some unit of IR.\n904:   ///\n905:   /// This pass can be run over any unit of IR and use any analysis manager\n906:   /// provided they satisfy the basic API requirements. When this pass is\n907:   /// created, these methods can be instantiated to satisfy whatever the\n908:   /// context requires.\n909:   PreservedAnalyses run(IRUnitT &Arg, AnalysisManagerT &AM,\n910:                         ExtraArgTs &&... Args) {\n911:     (void)AM.template getResult<AnalysisT>(Arg,\n912:                                            std::forward<ExtraArgTs>(Args)...);\n913: \n914:     return PreservedAnalyses::all();\n915:   }\n916:   void printPipeline(raw_ostream &OS,\n917:                      function_ref<StringRef(StringRef)> MapClassName2PassName) {\n918:     auto ClassName = AnalysisT::name();\n919:     auto PassName = MapClassName2PassName(ClassName);\n920:     OS << \"require<\" << PassName << '>';\n921:   }\n922:   static bool isRequired() { return true; }\n923: };\n924: \n925: /// A no-op pass template which simply forces a specific analysis result\n926: /// to be invalidated.\n927: template <typename AnalysisT>\n928: struct InvalidateAnalysisPass\n929:     : PassInfoMixin<InvalidateAnalysisPass<AnalysisT>> {\n930:   /// Run this pass over some unit of IR.\n931:   ///\n932:   /// This pass can be run over any unit of IR and use any analysis manager,\n933:   /// provided they satisfy the basic API requirements. ExtraArgTs>\n937:   PreservedAnalyses run(IRUnitT &Arg, AnalysisManagerT &AM, ExtraArgTs &&...) {\n938:     auto PA = PreservedAnalyses::all();\n939:     PA.abandon<AnalysisT>();\n940:     return PA;\n941:   }\n942:   void printPipeline(raw_ostream &OS,\n943:                      function_ref<StringRef(StringRef)> MapClassName2PassName) {\n944:     auto ClassName = AnalysisT::name();\n945:     auto PassName = MapClassName2PassName(ClassName);\n946:     OS << \"invalidate<\" << PassName << '>';\n947:   }\n948: };\n949: \n950: /// A utility pass that does nothing, but preserves no analyses.\n951: ///\n952: /// Because this preserves no analyses, any analysis passes queried after this\n953: /// pass runs will recompute fresh results.\n954: struct InvalidateAllAnalysesPass : PassInfoMixin<InvalidateAllAnalysesPass> {\n955:   /// Run this pass over some unit of IR.\n956:   template <typename IRUnitT, typename AnalysisManagerT, typename... ExtraArgTs>\n957:   PreservedAnalyses run(IRUnitT &, AnalysisManagerT &, ExtraArgTs &&...) {\n958:     return PreservedAnalyses::none();\n959:   }\n960: };\n961: \n962: } // end namespace llvm\n963: \n964: #endif // LLVM_IR_PASSMANAGER_H\n965: ", "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h"}, "isError": false}

Much like the function simplification pipeline above, it is\\n213:   /// suitable to run repeatedly over the IR and is not expected to destroy\\n214:   /// important information. All passes\\n331:   /// are comma separated. As a special shortcut, if the very first pass is not\\n332:   /// a module pass (as a module pass manager is), this will automatically form\\n333:   /// the shortest stack of pass managers that allow inserting that first pass.\\n334:   /// So, assuming function passes 'fpassN', CGSCC passes 'cgpassN', and loop\\n335:   /// passes 'lpassN', all of these are valid:\\n336:   ///\\n337:   ///   fpass1,fpass2,fpass3\\n338:   ///   cgpass1,cgpass2,cgpass3\\n339:   ///   lpass1,lpass2,lpass3\\n340:   ///\\n341:   /// And they are equivalent to the following (resp.):\\n342:   ///\\n343:   ///   module(function(fpass1,fpass2,fpass3))\\n344:   ///   module(cgscc(cgpass1,cgpass2,cgpass3))\\n345:   ///   module(function(loop(lpass1,lpass2,lpass3)))\\n346:   ///\\n347:   /// This shortcut is especially useful for debugging and testing small pass\\n348:   /// combinations.\\n349:   ///\\n350:   /// The sequence of passes aren't necessarily the exact same kind of pass.\\n351:   /// You can mix different levels implicitly if adaptor passes are defined to\\n352:   /// make them work. It is intended to be used\\n869: /// during parsing of a pass pipeline when parsing a single PipelineName.\\n870: /// When registering a new function analysis FancyAnalysis with the pass\\n871: /// pipeline name \\\"fancy-analysis\\\", a matching ParsePipelineCallback could look\\n872: /// like this:\\n873: ///\\n874: /// static bool parseFunctionPipeline(StringRef Name, FunctionPassManager \\u0026FPM,\\n875: ///                                   ArrayRef\\u003cPipelineElement\\u003e P) {\\n876: ///   if (parseAnalysisUtilityPasses\\u003cFancyAnalysis\\u003e(\\\"fancy-analysis\\\", Name,\\n877: ///                                                 FPM))\\n878: ///     return true;\\n879: ///   return false;\\n880: /// }\\n881: template \\u003ctypename AnalysisT, typename IRUnitT, typename AnalysisManagerT,\\n882:           typename... ExtraArgTs\\u003e\\n883: bool parseAnalysisUtilityPasses(\\n884:     StringRef AnalysisName, StringRef PipelineName,\\n885:     PassManager\\u003cIRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...\\u003e \\u0026PM) {\\n886:   if (!PipelineName.ends_with(\\\"\\u003e\\\"))\\n887:     return false;\\n888:   // See if this is an invalidate\\u003c\\u003e pass name\\n889:   if (PipelineName.starts_with(\\\"invalidate\\u003c\\\")) {\\n890:     PipelineName = PipelineName.substr(11, PipelineName.size() - 12);\\n891:     if (PipelineName != AnalysisName)\\n892:       return false;\\n893:     PM.addPass(InvalidateAnalysisPass\\u003cAnalysisT\\u003e());\\n894:     return true;\\n895:   }\\n896: \\n897:   // See if this is a require\\u003c\\u003e pass name\\n898:   if (PipelineName.starts_with(\\\"require\\u003c\\\")) {\\n899:     PipelineName = PipelineName.substr(8, PipelineName.size() - 9);\\n900:     if (PipelineName != AnalysisName)\\n901:       return false;\\n902:     PM.addPass(RequireAnalysisPass\\u003cAnalysisT, IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT,\\n903:                                    ExtraArgTs...\\u003e());\\n904:     return true;\\n905: configurations, and parsing of pass pipelines.\n12: ///\n13: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n14: \n15: #ifndef LLVM_PASSES_PASSBUILDER_H\n16: #define \"llvm/Transforms/IPO/ModuleInliner.h\"\n30: #include \"llvm/Transforms/Scalar/LoopPassManager.h\"\n31: #include <optional>\n32: #include <vector>\n33: \n34: namespace llvm {\n35: class StringRef;\n36: class AAManager;\n37: class TargetMachine;\n38: class ModuleSummaryIndex;\n39: \n40: /// Tunable parameters for passes in the default pipelines.\n41: class PipelineTuningOptions {\n42: public:\n43:   /// Constructor sets pipeline tuning defaults based on cl::opts. Each option\n44:   /// can be set in the PassBuilder when using a LLVM as a library.\n45:   LLVM_ABI PipelineTuningOptions();\n46: \n47:   /// Tuning option to set loop interleaving on/off, set based on opt level.\n48:   bool LoopInterleaving;\n49: \n50:   /// Tuning option to enable/disable loop vectorization, set based on opt\n51:   /// level.\n52:   bool LoopVectorization;\n53: \n54:   /// Tuning option to enable/disable slp loop vectorization, set based on opt\n55:   /// level.\n56:   bool SLPVectorization;\n57: \n58:   /// Tuning option to enable/disable loop unrolling. Its default value is true.\n59:   bool LoopUnrolling;\n60: \n61:   /// Tuning option to enable/disable loop interchange. Its default value is\n62:   /// false.\n63:   bool LoopInterchange;\n64: \n65:   /// Tuning option to enable/disable loop fusion. Its default value is false.\n66:   bool LoopFusion;\n67: \n68:   /// Tuning option to forget all SCEV loops in LoopUnroll. Its default value\n69:   /// is that of the flag: `-forget-scev-loop-unroll`.\n70:   bool ForgetAllSCEVInLoopUnroll;\n71: \n72:   /// Tuning option to cap the number of calls to retrive clobbering accesses in\n73:   /// MemorySSA, in LICM.\n74:   unsigned LicmMssaOptCap;\n75: \n76:   /// Tuning option to disable promotion to scalars in LICM with MemorySSA, if\n77:   /// the number of access is too large.\n78:   unsigned LicmMssaNoAccForPromotionCap;\n79: \n80:   /// Tuning option to enable/disable call graph profile. Its default value is\n81:   /// that of the flag: `-enable-npm-call-graph-profile`.\n82:   bool CallGraphProfile;\n83: \n84:   // Add LTO pipeline tuning option to enable the unified LTO pipeline.\n85:   bool UnifiedLTO;\n86: \n87:   /// Tuning option to enable/disable function merging. Its default value is\n88:   /// false.\n89:   bool MergeFunctions;\n90: \n91:   /// Tuning option to override the default inliner threshold.\n92:   int InlinerThreshold;\n93: \n94:   // Experimental option to eagerly invalidate more analyses. This has the\n95:   // potential to decrease max memory usage in exchange for more compile time.\n96:   // This may affect codegen due to either passes using analyses only when\n97:   // cached, or invalidating and recalculating an analysis that was\n98:   // stale/imprecise but still valid. Currently this invalidates all function\n99:   // analyses after various module->function or cgscc->function adaptors in the\n100:   // default pipelines.\n101:   bool EagerlyInvalidateAnalyses;\n102: \n103:   // Tuning option to enable/disable speculative devirtualization.\n104:   // Its default value is false.\n105:   bool DevirtualizeSpeculatively;\n106: };\n107: \n108: /// This class provides access to building LLVM's passes.\n109: ///\n110: /// Its members provide the baseline state available to passes during their\n111: /// construction. The \\c PassRegistry.def file specifies how to construct all\n112: /// of the built-in passes, and those may reference these members during\n113: /// construction.\n114: class PassBuilder {\n115:   TargetMachine *TM;\n116:   PipelineTuningOptions PTO;\n117:   std::optional<PGOOptions> PGOOpt;\n118:   PassInstrumentationCallbacks *PIC;\n119:   IntrusiveRefCntPtr<vfs::FileSystem> FS;\n120: \n121: public:\n122:   /// A struct to capture parsed pass pipeline names.\n123:   ///\n124:   /// A pipeline is defined as a series of names, each of which may in itself\n125:   /// recursively contain a nested pipeline. A name is either the name of a pass\n126:   /// (e.g. \"instcombine\") or the name of a pipeline type (e.g. \"cgscc\"). If the\n127:   /// name is the name of a pass, the InnerPipeline is empty, since passes\n128:   /// cannot contain inner pipelines. See parsePassPipeline() for a more\n129:   /// detailed description of the textual pipeline format.\n130:   struct PipelineElement {\n131:     StringRef Name;\n132:     std::vector<PipelineElement> InnerPipeline;\n133:   };\n134: \n135:   LLVM_ABI explicit PassBuilder(\n136:       TargetMachine *TM = nullptr,\n137:       PipelineTuningOptions PTO = PipelineTuningOptions(),\n138:       std::optional<PGOOptions> PGOOpt = std::nullopt,\n139:       PassInstrumentationCallbacks *PIC = nullptr,\n140:       IntrusiveRefCntPtr<vfs::FileSystem> FS = vfs::getRealFileSystem());\n141: \n142:   /// Cross register the analysis managers through their proxies.\n143:   ///\n144:   /// This is an interface that can be used to cross register each\n145:   /// AnalysisManager with all the others analysis managers.\n146:   LLVM_ABI void\n147:   crossRegisterProxies(LoopAnalysisManager &LAM, FunctionAnalysisManager &FAM,\n148:                        CGSCCAnalysisManager &CGAM, ModuleAnalysisManager &MAM,\n149:                        MachineFunctionAnalysisManager *MFAM = nullptr);\n150: \n151:   /// Registers all available module analysis passes.\n152:   ///\n153:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \\c\n154:   /// ModuleAnalysisManager with all registered module analyses. Callers can\n155:   /// still manually register any additional analyses. Callers can also\n156:   /// pre-register analyses and this will not override those.\n157:   LLVM_ABI void registerModuleAnalyses(ModuleAnalysisManager &MAM);\n158: \n159:   /// Registers all available CGSCC analysis passes.\n160:   ///\n161:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \\c CGSCCAnalysisManager\n162:   /// with all registered CGSCC analyses. Callers can still manually register any\n163:   /// additional analyses. Callers can also pre-register analyses and this will\n164:   /// not override those.\n165:   LLVM_ABI void registerCGSCCAnalyses(CGSCCAnalysisManager &CGAM);\n166: \n167:   /// Registers all available function analysis passes.\n168:   ///\n169:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \\c\n170:   /// FunctionAnalysisManager with all registered function analyses. Callers can\n171:   /// still manually register any additional analyses. Callers can also\n172:   /// pre-register analyses and this will not override those.\n173:   LLVM_ABI void registerFunctionAnalyses(FunctionAnalysisManager &FAM);\n174: \n175:   /// Registers all available loop analysis passes.\n176:   ///\n177:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \\c LoopAnalysisManager\n178:   /// with all registered loop analyses. Callers can still manually register any\n179:   /// additional analyses.\n180:   LLVM_ABI void registerLoopAnalyses(LoopAnalysisManager &LAM);\n181: \n182:   /// Registers all available machine function analysis passes.\n183:   ///\n184:   /// This is an interface that can be used to populate a \\c\n185:   /// MachineFunctionAnalysisManager with all registered function analyses.\n186:   /// Callers can still manually register any additional analyses. Callers can\n187:   /// also pre-register analyses and this will not override those.\n188:   LLVM_ABI void\n189:   registerMachineFunctionAnalyses(MachineFunctionAnalysisManager &MFAM);\n190: \n191:   /// Construct the core LLVM function canonicalization and simplification\n192:   /// pipeline.\n193:   ///\n194:   /// This is a long pipeline and uses most of the per-function optimization\n195:   /// passes in LLVM to canonicalize and simplify the IR. It is suitable to run\n196:   /// repeatedly over the IR and is not expected to destroy important\n197:   /// information about the semantics of the IR.\n198:   ///\n199:   /// Note that \\p Level cannot be `O0` here. The pipelines produced are\n200:   /// only intended for use when attempting to optimize code. If frontends\n201:   /// require some transformations for semantic reasons, they should explicitly\n202:   /// build them.\n203:   ///\n204:   /// \\p Phase indicates the current ThinLTO phase.\n205:   LLVM_ABI FunctionPassManager buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline(\n206:       OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);\n207: \n208:   /// Construct the core LLVM module canonicalization and simplification\n209:   /// pipeline.\n210:   ///\n211:   /// This pipeline focuses on canonicalizing and simplifying the entire module\n212:   /// of IR. Much like the function simplification pipeline above, it is\n213:   /// suitable to run repeatedly over the IR and is not expected to destroy\n214:   /// important information. It does, however, perform inlining and other\n215:   /// heuristic based simplifications that are not strictly reversible.\n216:   ///\n217:   /// Note that \\p Level cannot be `O0` here. The pipelines produced are\n218:   /// only intended for use when attempting to optimize code. If frontends\n219:   /// require some transformations for semantic reasons, they should explicitly\n220:   /// build them.\n221:   ///\n222:   /// \\p Phase indicates the current ThinLTO phase.\n223:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager buildModuleSimplificationPipeline(\n224:       OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);\n225: \n226:   /// Construct the module pipeline that performs inlining as well as\n227:   /// the inlining-driven cleanups.\n228:   LLVM_ABI ModuleInlinerWrapperPass\n229:   buildInlinerPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);\n230: \n231:   /// Construct the module pipeline that performs inlining with\n232:   /// module inliner pass.\n233:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager\n234:   buildModuleInlinerPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase);\n235: \n236:   /// Construct the core LLVM module optimization pipeline.\n237:   ///\n238:   /// This pipeline focuses on optimizing the execution speed of the IR. It\n239:   /// uses cost modeling and thresholds to balance code growth against runtime\n240:   /// improvements. It includes vectorization and other information destroying\n241:   /// transformations. It also cannot generally be run repeatedly on a module\n242:   /// without potentially seriously regressing either runtime performance of\n243:   /// the code or serious code size growth.\n244:   ///\n245:   /// Note that \\p Level cannot be `O0` here. The pipelines produced are\n246:   /// only intended for use when attempting to optimize code. If frontends\n247:   /// require some transformations for semantic reasons, they should explicitly\n248:   /// build them.\n249:   LLVM_ABI ModulePassManager buildModuleOptimizationPipeline(\n250:       OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase LTOPhase);\n251: \n252:   /// Build a per-module default optimization pipeline.\n253:   ///\n254:   /// This provides a good default optimization pipeline for per-module\n255:   /// optimization and code generation without any link-time optimization. All passes\n331:   /// are comma separated. As a special shortcut, if the very first pass is not\n332:   /// a module pass (as a module pass manager is), this will automatically form\n333:   /// the shortest stack of pass managers that allow inserting that first pass.\n334:   /// So, assuming function passes 'fpassN', CGSCC passes 'cgpassN', and loop\n335:   /// passes 'lpassN', all of these are valid:\n336:   ///\n337:   ///   fpass1,fpass2,fpass3\n338:   ///   cgpass1,cgpass2,cgpass3\n339:   ///   lpass1,lpass2,lpass3\n340:   ///\n341:   /// And they are equivalent to the following (resp.):\n342:   ///\n343:   ///   module(function(fpass1,fpass2,fpass3))\n344:   ///   module(cgscc(cgpass1,cgpass2,cgpass3))\n345:   ///   module(function(loop(lpass1,lpass2,lpass3)))\n346:   ///\n347:   /// This shortcut is especially useful for debugging and testing small pass\n348:   /// combinations.\n349:   ///\n350:   /// The sequence of passes aren't necessarily the exact same kind of pass.\n351:   /// You can mix different levels implicitly if adaptor passes are defined to\n352:   /// make them work. For example,\n353:   ///\n354:   ///   mpass1,fpass1,fpass2,mpass2,lpass1\n355:   ///\n356:   /// This pipeline uses only one pass manager: the top-level module manager.\n357:   /// fpass1,fpass2 and lpass1 are added into the top-level module manager\n358:   /// using only adaptor passes. No nested function/loop pass managers are\n359:   /// added. The purpose is to allow easy pass testing when the user\n360:   /// specifically want the pass to run under a adaptor directly. utility template takes care of adding require<> and invalidate<>\n868: /// passes for an analysis to a given \\c PassManager. It is intended to be used\n869: /// during parsing of a pass pipeline when parsing a single PipelineName.\n870: /// When registering a new function analysis FancyAnalysis with the pass\n871: /// pipeline name \"fancy-analysis\", a matching ParsePipelineCallback could look\n872: /// like this:\n873: ///\n874: /// static bool parseFunctionPipeline(StringRef Name, FunctionPassManager &FPM,\n875: ///                                   ArrayRef<PipelineElement> P) {\n876: ///   if (parseAnalysisUtilityPasses<FancyAnalysis>(\"fancy-analysis\", Name,\n877: ///                                                 FPM))\n878: ///     return true;\n879: ///   return false;\n880: /// }\n881: template <typename AnalysisT, typename IRUnitT, typename AnalysisManagerT,\n882:           typename... ExtraArgTs>\n883: bool parseAnalysisUtilityPasses(\n884:     StringRef AnalysisName, StringRef PipelineName,\n885:     PassManager<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...> &PM) {\n886:   if (!PipelineName.ends_with(\">\"))\n887:     return false;\n888:   // See if this is an invalidate<> pass name\n889:   if (PipelineName.starts_with(\"invalidate<\")) {\n890:     PipelineName = PipelineName.substr(11, PipelineName.size() - 12);\n891:     if (PipelineName != AnalysisName)\n892:       return false;\n893:     PM.addPass(InvalidateAnalysisPass<AnalysisT>());\n894:     return true;\n895:   }\n896: \n897:   // See if this is a require<> pass name\n898:   if (PipelineName.starts_with(\"require<\")) {\n899:     PipelineName = PipelineName.substr(8, PipelineName.size() - 9);\n900:     if (PipelineName != AnalysisName)\n901:       return false;\n902:     PM.addPass(RequireAnalysisPass<AnalysisT, IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT,\n903:                                    ExtraArgTs...>());\n904:     return true;\n905:   }\n906: \n907:   return false;\n908: }\n909: \n910: // These are special since they are only for testing purposes.\n911: \n912: /// No-op module pass which does nothing.\n913: struct NoOpModulePass : PassInfoMixin<NoOpModulePass> {\n914:   PreservedAnalyses run(Module &M, ModuleAnalysisManager &) {\n915:     return PreservedAnalyses::all();\n916:   }\n917: };\n918: \n919: /// No-op module analysis.\n920: class NoOpModuleAnalysis : public AnalysisInfoMixin<NoOpModuleAnalysis> {\n921:   friend AnalysisInfoMixin<NoOpModuleAnalysis>;\n922:   LLVM_ABI static AnalysisKey Key;\n923: \n924: public:\n925:   struct Result {};\n926:   Result run(Module &, ModuleAnalysisManager &) { return Result(); }\n927: };\n928: \n929: /// No-op CGSCC pass which does nothing.\n930: struct NoOpCGSCCPass : PassInfoMixin<NoOpCGSCCPass> {\n931:   PreservedAnalyses run(LazyCallGraph::SCC &C, CGSCCAnalysisManager &,\n932:                         LazyCallGraph &, CGSCCUpdateResult &UR) {\n933:     return PreservedAnalyses::all();\n934:   }\n935: };\n936: \n937: /// No-op CGSCC analysis.\n938: class NoOpCGSCCAnalysis : public AnalysisInfoMixin<NoOpCGSCCAnalysis> {\n939:   friend AnalysisInfoMixin<NoOpCGSCCAnalysis>;\n940:   LLVM_ABI static AnalysisKey Key;\n941: \n942: public:\n943:   struct Result {};\n944:   Result run(LazyCallGraph::SCC &, CGSCCAnalysisManager &, LazyCallGraph &G) {\n945:     return Result();\n946:   }\n947: };\n948: \n949: /// No-op function pass which does nothing.\n950: struct NoOpFunctionPass : PassInfoMixin<NoOpFunctionPass> {\n951:   PreservedAnalyses run(Function &F, FunctionAnalysisManager &) {\n952:     return PreservedAnalyses::all();\n953:   }\n954: };\n955: \n956: /// No-op function analysis.\n957: class NoOpFunctionAnalysis : public AnalysisInfoMixin<NoOpFunctionAnalysis> {\n958:   friend AnalysisInfoMixin<NoOpFunctionAnalysis>;\n959:   LLVM_ABI static AnalysisKey Key;\n960: \n961: public:\n962:   struct Result {};\n963:   Result run(Function &, FunctionAnalysisManager &) { return Result(); }\n964: };\n965: \n966: /// No-op loop nest pass which does nothing.\n967: struct NoOpLoopNestPass : PassInfoMixin<NoOpLoopNestPass> {\n968:   PreservedAnalyses run(LoopNest &L, LoopAnalysisManager &,\n969:                         LoopStandardAnalysisResults &, LPMUpdater &) {\n970:     return PreservedAnalyses::all();\n971:   }\n972: };\n973: \n974: /// No-op loop pass which does nothing.\n975: struct NoOpLoopPass : PassInfoMixin<NoOpLoopPass> {\n976:   PreservedAnalyses run(Loop &L, LoopAnalysisManager &,\n977:                         LoopStandardAnalysisResults &, LPMUpdater &) {\n978:     return PreservedAnalyses::all();\n979:   }\n980: };\n981: \n982: /// No-op machine function pass which does nothing.\n983: struct NoOpMachineFunctionPass : public PassInfoMixin<NoOpMachineFunctionPass> {\n984:   PreservedAnalyses run(MachineFunction &, MachineFunctionAnalysisManager &) {\n985:     return PreservedAnalyses::all();\n986:   }\n987: };\n988: \n989: /// No-op loop analysis.\n990: class NoOpLoopAnalysis : public AnalysisInfoMixin<NoOpLoopAnalysis> {\n991:   friend AnalysisInfoMixin<NoOpLoopAnalysis>;\n992:   LLVM_ABI static AnalysisKey Key;\n993: \n994: public:\n995:   struct Result {};\n996:   Result run(Loop &, LoopAnalysisManager &, LoopStandardAnalysisResults &) {\n997:     return Result();\n998:   }\n999: };\n1000: \n1001: /// Common option used by multiple tools to print pipeline passes\n1002: LLVM_ABI extern cl::opt<bool> PrintPipelinePasses;\n1003: }\n1004: \n1005: #endif\n1006: ", "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h"}, "isError": false}

If you want to use LLVM on multiple\n36: /// threads simultaneously, you will need to use a separate PassRegistry on\n37: /// each thread.\n38: class PassRegistry {\n39:   mutable sys::SmartRWMutex\u003ctrue\u003e Lock;\n40: \n41:   /// PassInfoMap - Keep track of the PassInfo object for each registered pass.\n42:   using MapType = DenseMap\u003cconst void *, const PassInfo *\u003e;\n43:   MapType PassInfoMap;\n44: \n45:   using StringMapType = StringMap\u003cconst PassInfo *\u003e;\n46:   StringMapType PassInfoStringMap;\n47: \n48:   std::vector\u003cstd::unique_ptr\u003cconst PassInfo\u003e\u003e ToFree;\n49:   std::vector\u003cPassRegistrationListener *\u003e Listeners;\n50: \n51: public:\n52:   PassRegistry() = default;\n53:   LLVM_ABI ~PassRegistry();\n54: \n55:   /// getPassRegistry - Access the global registry object, which is\n56:   /// automatically initialized at application launch and destroyed by\n57:   /// llvm_shutdown.\n58:   LLVM_ABI static PassRegistry *getPassRegistry();\n59: \n60:   /// getPassInfo - Look up a pass' corresponding PassInfo, indexed by the pass'\n61:   /// type identifier (\u0026MyPass::ID).\n62:   LLVM_ABI const PassInfo *getPassInfo(const void *TI) const;\n63: \n64:   /// getPassInfo - Look up a pass' corresponding PassInfo, indexed by the pass'\n65:   /// argument string.\n66:   LLVM_ABI const PassInfo *getPassInfo(StringRef Arg) const;\n67: \n68:   /// registerPass - Register a pass (by means of its PassInfo) with the\n69:   /// registry.  Required in order to use the pass with a PassManager.\n70:   LLVM_ABI void registerPass(const PassInfo \u0026PI, bool ShouldFree = false);\n71: \n72:   /// enumerateWith - Enumerate the registered passes, calling the provided\n73:   /// PassRegistrationListener's passEnumerate() callback on each of them.\n74:   LLVM_ABI void enumerateWith(PassRegistrationListener *L);\n75: \n76:   /// addRegistrationListener - Register the given PassRegistrationListener\n77:   /// to receive passRegistered() callbacks whenever a new pass is registered.\n78:   LLVM_ABI void addRegistrationListener(PassRegistrationListener *L);\n79: \n80:   /// removeRegistrationListener - Unregister a PassRegistrationListener so that\n81:   /// it no longer receives passRegistered() callbacks.\n82:   LLVM_ABI void removeRegistrationListener(PassRegistrationListener *L);\n83: };\n84: \n85: } // end namespace llvm\n86: \n87: #endif // LLVM_PASSREGISTRY_H\n88: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h"}", "annotations": null, "meta": null}], "structuredContent": {"content": "1: //===- llvm/PassRegistry.h - Pass Information Registry ----------*- C++ -*-===//
2: //
3: // Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.
4: // See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.
5: // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception
6: //
7: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//
8: //
9: // This file defines PassRegistry, a class that is used in the initialization
10: // and registration of passes.  At application startup, passes are registered
11: // with the PassRegistry, which is later provided to the PassManager for
12: // dependency resolution and similar tasks.
13: //
14: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//
15: 
16: #ifndef LLVM_PASSREGISTRY_H
17: #define LLVM_PASSREGISTRY_H
18: 
19: #include "llvm/ADT/DenseMap.h"
20: #include "llvm/ADT/StringMap.h"
21: #include "llvm/ADT/StringRef.h"
22: #include "llvm/Support/Compiler.h"
23: #include "llvm/Support/RWMutex.h"
24: #include <memory>
25: #include <vector>
26: 
27: namespace llvm {
28: 
29: class PassInfo;
30: struct PassRegistrationListener;
31: 
32: /// PassRegistry - This class manages the registration and intitialization of
33: /// the pass subsystem as application startup, and assists the PassManager
34: /// in resolving pass dependencies.
35: /// NOTE: PassRegistry is NOT thread-safe.  If you want to use LLVM on multiple
36: /// threads simultaneously, you will need to use a separate PassRegistry on
37: /// each thread.
38: class PassRegistry {
39:   mutable sys::SmartRWMutex<true> Lock;
40: 
41:   /// PassInfoMap - Keep track of the PassInfo object for each registered pass.
42:   using MapType = DenseMap<const void *, const PassInfo *>;
43:   MapType PassInfoMap;
44: 
45:   using StringMapType = StringMap<const PassInfo *>;
46:   StringMapType PassInfoStringMap;
47: 
48:   std::vector<std::unique_ptr<const PassInfo>> ToFree;
49:   std::vector<PassRegistrationListener *> Listeners;
50: 
51: public:
52:   PassRegistry() = default;
53:   LLVM_ABI ~PassRegistry();
54: 
55:   /// getPassRegistry - Access the global registry object, which is
56:   /// automatically initialized at application launch and destroyed by
57:   /// llvm_shutdown.
58:   LLVM_ABI static PassRegistry *getPassRegistry();
59: 
60:   /// getPassInfo - Look up a pass' corresponding PassInfo, indexed by the pass'
61:   /// type identifier (&MyPass::ID).
62:   LLVM_ABI const PassInfo *getPassInfo(const void *TI) const;
63: 
64:   /// getPassInfo - Look up a pass' corresponding PassInfo, indexed by the pass'
65:   /// argument string.
66:   LLVM_ABI const PassInfo *getPassInfo(StringRef Arg) const;
67: 
68:   /// registerPass - Register a pass (by means of its PassInfo) with the
69:   /// registry.  Required in order to use the pass with a PassManager.
70:   LLVM_ABI void registerPass(const PassInfo &PI, bool ShouldFree = false);
71: 
72:   /// enumerateWith - Enumerate the registered passes, calling the provided
73:   /// PassRegistrationListener's passEnumerate() callback on each of them.
74:   LLVM_ABI void enumerateWith(PassRegistrationListener *L);
75: 
76:   /// addRegistrationListener - Register the given PassRegistrationListener
77:   /// to receive passRegistered() callbacks whenever a new pass is registered.
78:   LLVM_ABI void addRegistrationListener(PassRegistrationListener *L);
79: 
80:   /// removeRegistrationListener - Unregister a PassRegistrationListener so that
81:   /// it no longer receives passRegistered() callbacks.
82:   LLVM_ABI void removeRegistrationListener(PassRegistrationListener *L);
83: };
84: 
85: } // end namespace llvm
86: 
87: #endif // LLVM_PASSREGISTRY_H

This file\n10: // is automatically #included by Pass.h, so:\n11: //\n12: //           NO .CPP FILES SHOULD INCLUDE THIS FILE DIRECTLY\n13: //\n14: // Instead, #include Pass.h.\n15: //\n16: // This file defines Pass registration code and classes used for it.\n17: //\n18: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n19: \n20: #if !defined(LLVM_PASS_H) || defined(LLVM_PASSSUPPORT_H)\n21: #error \"Do not include \u003cPassSupport.h\u003e; include \u003cPass.h\u003e instead\"\n22: #endif\n23: \n24: #ifndef LLVM_PASSSUPPORT_H\n25: #define LLVM_PASSSUPPORT_H\n26: \n27: #include \"llvm/ADT/StringRef.h\"\n28: #include \"llvm/PassInfo.h\"\n29: #include \"llvm/PassRegistry.h\"\n30: #include \"llvm/Support/Compiler.h\"\n31: #include \"llvm/Support/Error.h\"\n32: #include \"llvm/Support/Threading.h\"\n33: #include \u003cfunctional\u003e\n34: \n35: namespace llvm {\n36: \n37: class Pass;\n38: \n39: #define INITIALIZE_PASS_BEGIN(passName, arg, name, cfg, analysis)              \\\n40:   static void initialize##passName##PassOnce(PassRegistry \u0026Registry) {\n41: \n42: #define INITIALIZE_PASS_DEPENDENCY(depName) initialize##depName##Pass(Registry);\n43: \n44: #define INITIALIZE_PASS_END(passName, arg, name, cfg, analysis)                \\\n45:   PassInfo *PI = new PassInfo(                                                 \\\n46:       name, arg, \u0026passName::ID,                                                \\\n47:       PassInfo::NormalCtor_t(callDefaultCtor\u003cpassName\u003e), cfg, analysis);       \\\n48:   Registry.registerPass(*PI, true);                                            \\\n49:   }                                                                            \\\n50:   static llvm::once_flag Initialize##passName##PassFlag;                       \\\n51:   void llvm::initialize##passName##Pass(PassRegistry \u0026Registry) {              \\\n52:     llvm::call_once(Initialize##passName##PassFlag,                            \\\n53:                     initialize##passName##PassOnce, std::ref(Registry)); 8: //
9: // This file defines stuff that is used to define and "use" Passes.  This file
10: // is automatically #included by Pass.h, so:
11: //
12: //           NO .CPP FILES SHOULD INCLUDE THIS FILE DIRECTLY
13: //
14: // Instead, #include Pass.h.
15: //
16: // This file defines Pass registration code and classes used for it.
17: //
18: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//
19: 
20: #if !defined(LLVM_PASS_H) || defined(LLVM_PASSSUPPORT_H)
21: #error "Do not include <PassSupport.h>; include <Pass.h> instead"
22: #endif
23: 
24: #ifndef LLVM_PASSSUPPORT_H
25: #define LLVM_PASSSUPPORT_H
26:
