28: #include "llvm/PassInfo.h"
29: #include "llvm/PassRegistry.h"
30: #include "llvm/Support/Compiler.h"
31: #include "llvm/Support/Error.h"
32: #include "llvm/Support/Threading.h"
33: #include <functional>
34: 
35: namespace llvm {
36: 
37: class Pass;
38: 
39: #define INITIALIZE_PASS_BEGIN(passName, arg, name, cfg, analysis)              \
40:   static void initialize##passName##PassOnce(PassRegistry &Registry) {
41: 
42: #define INITIALIZE_PASS_DEPENDENCY(depName) initialize##depName##Pass(Registry);
43: 
44: #define INITIALIZE_PASS_END(passName, arg, name, cfg, analysis)                \
45:   PassInfo *PI = new PassInfo(                                                 \
46:       name, arg, &passName::ID,                                                \
47:       PassInfo::NormalCtor_t(callDefaultCtor<passName>), cfg, analysis);       \
48:   Registry.registerPass(*PI, true);                                            \
49:   }                                                                            \
50:   static llvm::once_flag Initialize##passName##PassFlag;                       \
51:   void llvm::initialize##passName##Pass(PassRegistry &Registry) {              \
52:     llvm::call_once(Initialize##passName##PassFlag,                            \
53:                     initialize##passName##PassOnce, std::ref(Registry));       \
54:   }
55: 
56: #define INITIALIZE_PASS(passName, arg, name, cfg, analysis)                    \
57:   INITIALIZE_PASS_BEGIN(passName, arg, name, cfg, analysis)                    \
58:   INITIALIZE_PASS_END(passName, arg, name, cfg, analysis)
59: 
60: #define INITIALIZE_PASS_WITH_OPTIONS_BEGIN(PassName, Arg, Name, Cfg, Analysis) \
61:   INITIALIZE_PASS_BEGIN(PassName, Arg, Name, Cfg, Analysis)                    \
62:   PassName::registerOptions();
63: 
64: #define INITIALIZE_PASS_WITH_OPTIONS(PassName, Arg, Name, Cfg, Analysis)       \
65:   INITIALIZE_PASS_WITH_OPTIONS_BEGIN(PassName, Arg, Name, Cfg, Analysis)       \
66:   INITIALIZE_PASS_END(PassName, Arg, Name, Cfg, Analysis)
67: 
68: template <class PassName> Pass *callDefaultCtor() {
69:   if constexpr (std::is_default_constructible_v<PassName>)
70:     return new PassName();
71:   else
72:     // Some codegen passes should only be testable via
73:     // `llc -{start|stop}-{before|after}=<passname>`, not via `opt -<passname>`.
74:     report_fatal_error("target-specific codegen-only pass");
75: }
76: 
77: //===---------------------------------------------------------------------------
78: /// RegisterPass<t> template - This template class is used to notify the system
79: /// that a Pass is available for use, and registers it into the internal
80: /// database maintained by the PassManager.  Unless this template is used, opt,
81: /// for example will not be able to see the pass and attempts to create the pass
82: /// will fail. This template is used in the follow manner (at global scope, in
83: /// your .cpp file):
84: ///
85: /// static RegisterPass<YourPassClassName> tmp("passopt", "My Pass Name");
86: ///
87: /// This statement will cause your pass to be created by calling the default
88: /// constructor exposed by the pass.
89: template <typename passName> struct RegisterPass : public PassInfo {
90:   // Register Pass using default constructor...
91:   RegisterPass(StringRef PassArg, StringRef Name, bool CFGOnly = false,
92:                bool is_analysis = false)
93:       : PassInfo(Name, PassArg, &passName::ID,
94:                  PassInfo::NormalCtor_t(callDefaultCtor<passName>), CFGOnly,
95:                  is_analysis) {
96:     PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->registerPass(*this);
97:   }
98: };
99: 
100: //===---------------------------------------------------------------------------
101: /// PassRegistrationListener class - This class is meant to be derived from by
102: /// clients that are interested in which passes get registered and unregistered
103: /// at runtime (which can be because of the RegisterPass constructors being run
104: /// as the program starts up, or may be because a shared object just got
105: /// loaded).
106: struct PassRegistrationListener {
107:   PassRegistrationListener() = default;
108:   virtual ~PassRegistrationListener() = default;
109: 
110:   /// Callback functions - These functions are invoked whenever a pass is loaded
111:   /// or removed from the current executable.
112:   virtual void passRegistered(const PassInfo *) {}
113: 
114:   /// enumeratePasses - Iterate over the registered passes, calling the
115:   /// passEnumerate callback on each PassInfo object.
116:   LLVM_ABI void enumeratePasses();
117: 
118:   /// passEnumerate - Callback function invoked when someone calls
119:   /// enumeratePasses on this PassRegistrationListener object.
120:   virtual void passEnumerate(const PassInfo *) {}
121: };
122: 
123: } // end namespace llvm
124: 
125: #endif // LLVM_PASSSUPPORT_H

{"meta": null, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{"content":"1: //===-------- LLVM-provided High-Level Optimization levels -*- C++ -*------===//\n2: //\n3: // Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.\n4: // See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.\n5: // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception\n6: //\n7: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n8: /// \\file\n9: ///\n10: /// This header enumerates the LLVM-provided high-level optimization levels.\n11: /// Each level has a specific goal and rationale.\n12: ///\n13: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n14: \n15: #ifndef LLVM_PASSES_OPTIMIZATIONLEVEL_H\n16: #define LLVM_PASSES_OPTIMIZATIONLEVEL_H\n17: \n18: #include \"llvm/Support/Compiler.h\"\n19: #include \u003cassert.h\u003e\n20: \n21: namespace llvm {\n22: \n23: class OptimizationLevel final {\n24:   unsigned SpeedLevel = 2;\n25:   unsigned SizeLevel = 0;\n26:   OptimizationLevel(unsigned SpeedLevel, unsigned SizeLevel)\n27:       : SpeedLevel(SpeedLevel), SizeLevel(SizeLevel) {\n28:     // Check that only valid combinations are passed.\n29:     assert(SpeedLevel \u003c= 3 \u0026\u0026\n30:            \"Optimization level for speed should be 0, 1, 2, or 3\");\n31:     assert(SizeLevel \u003c= 2 \u0026\u0026\n32:            \"Optimization level for size should be 0, 1, or 2\");\n33:     assert((SizeLevel == 0 || SpeedLevel == 2) \u0026\u0026\n34:            \"Optimize for size should be encoded with speedup level == 2\");\n35:   }\n36: \n37: public:\n38:   OptimizationLevel() = default;\n39:   /// Disable as many optimizations as possible. This doesn't completely\n40:   /// disable the optimizer in all cases, for example always_inline functions\n41:   /// can be required to be inlined for correctness.\n42:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O0;\n43: \n44:   /// Optimize quickly without destroying debuggability.\n45:   ///\n46:   /// This level is tuned to produce a result from the optimizer as quickly\n47:   /// as possible and to avoid destroying debuggability. This tends to result\n48:   /// in a very good development mode where the compiled code will be\n49:   /// immediately executed as part of testing. As a consequence, where\n50:   /// possible, we would like to produce efficient-to-execute code, but not\n51:   /// if it significantly slows down compilation or would prevent even basic\n52:   /// debugging of the resulting binary.\n53:   ///\n54:   /// As an example, complex loop transformations such as versioning,\n55:   /// vectorization, or fusion don't make sense here due to the degree to\n56:   /// which the executed code differs from the source code, and the compile\n57:   /// time cost.\n58:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O1;\n59:   /// Optimize for fast execution as much as possible without triggering\n60:   /// significant incremental compile time or code size growth.\n61:   ///\n62:   /// The key idea is that optimizations at this level should \"pay for\n63:   /// themselves\". So if an optimization increases compile time by 5% or\n64:   /// increases code size by 5% for a particular benchmark, that benchmark\n65:   /// should also be one which sees a 5% runtime improvement. If the compile\n66:   /// time or code size penalties happen on average across a diverse range of\n67:   /// LLVM users' benchmarks, then the improvements should as well.\n68:   ///\n69:   /// And no matter what, the compile time needs to not grow superlinearly\n70:   /// with the size of input to LLVM so that users can control the runtime of\n71:   /// the optimizer in this mode.\n72:   ///\n73:   /// This is expected to be a good default optimization level for the vast\n74:   /// majority of users.\n75:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O2;\n76:   /// Optimize for fast execution as much as possible.\n77:   ///\n78:   /// This mode is significantly more aggressive in trading off compile time\n79:   /// and code size to get execution time improvements. The core idea is that\n80:   /// this mode should include any optimization that helps execution time on\n81:   /// balance across a diverse collection of benchmarks, even if it increases\n82:   /// code size or compile time for some benchmarks without corresponding\n83:   /// improvements to execution time.\n84:   ///\n85:   /// Despite being willing to trade more compile time off to get improved\n86:   /// execution time, this mode still tries to avoid superlinear growth in\n87:   /// order to make even significantly slower compile times at least scale\n88:   /// reasonably. This does not preclude very substantial constant factor\n89:   /// costs though.\n90:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O3;\n91:   /// Similar to \\c O2 but tries to optimize for small code size instead of\n92:   /// fast execution without triggering significant incremental execution\n93:   /// time slowdowns.\n94:   ///\n95:   /// The logic here is exactly the same as \\c O2, but with code size and\n96:   /// execution time metrics swapped.\n97:   ///\n98:   /// A consequence of the different core goal is that this should in general\n99:   /// produce substantially smaller executables that still run in\n100:   /// a reasonable amount of time.\n101:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel Os;\n102:   /// A very specialized mode that will optimize for code size at any and all\n103:   /// costs.\n104:   ///\n105:   /// This is useful primarily when there are absolute size limitations and\n106:   /// any effort taken to reduce the size is worth it regardless of the\n107:   /// execution time impact. You should expect this level to produce rather\n108:   /// slow, but very small, code.\n109:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel Oz;\n110: \n111:   bool isOptimizingForSpeed() const { return SizeLevel == 0 \u0026\u0026 SpeedLevel \u003e 0; }\n112: \n113:   bool isOptimizingForSize() const { return SizeLevel \u003e 0; }\n114: \n115:   bool operator==(const OptimizationLevel \u0026Other) const {\n116:     return SizeLevel == Other.SizeLevel \u0026\u0026 SpeedLevel == Other.SpeedLevel;\n117:   }\n118:   bool operator!=(const OptimizationLevel \u0026Other) const {\n119:     return SizeLevel != Other.SizeLevel || SpeedLevel != Other.SpeedLevel;\n120:   }\n121: \n122:   unsigned getSpeedupLevel() const { return SpeedLevel; }\n123: \n124:   unsigned getSizeLevel() const { return SizeLevel; }\n125: };\n126: } // namespace llvm\n127: \n128: #endif\n129: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/include/llvm/Passes/OptimizationLevel.h"}", "annotations": null, "meta": null}], "structuredContent": {"content": "1: //===-------- LLVM-provided High-Level Optimization levels -*- C++ -*------===//

23: class OptimizationLevel final {
24:   unsigned SpeedLevel = 2;

35:   }
36: 
37: public:
38:   OptimizationLevel() = default;
39:   /// Disable as many optimizations as possible. This doesn't completely
40:   /// disable the optimizer in all cases, for example always_inline functions
41:   /// can be required to be inlined for correctness.
42:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O0;
43: 
44:   /// Optimize quickly without destroying debuggability.
45:   ///
46:   /// This level is tuned to produce a result from the optimizer as quickly
47:   /// as possible and to avoid destroying debuggability. This tends to result
48:   /// in a very good development mode where the compiled code will be
49:   /// immediately executed as part of testing. As a consequence, where
50:   /// possible, we would like to produce efficient-to-execute code, but not
51:   /// if it significantly slows down compilation or would prevent even basic
52:   /// debugging of the resulting binary.
53:   ///
54:   /// As an example, complex loop transformations such as versioning,
55:   /// vectorization, or fusion don't make sense here due to the degree to
56:   /// which the executed code differs from the source code, and the compile
57:   /// time cost.
58:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O1;
59:   /// Optimize for fast execution as much as possible without triggering
60:   /// significant incremental compile time or code size growth.
61:   ///
62:   /// The key idea is that optimizations at this level should "pay for
63:   /// themselves". So if an optimization increases compile time by 5% or
64:   /// increases code size by 5% for a particular benchmark, that benchmark
65:   /// should also be one which sees a 5% runtime improvement. If the compile
66:   /// time or code size penalties happen on average across a diverse range of
67:   /// LLVM users' benchmarks, then the improvements should as well.
68:   ///
69:   /// And no matter what, the compile time needs to not grow superlinearly
70:   /// with the size of input to LLVM so that users can control the runtime of
71:   /// the optimizer in this mode.
72:   ///
73:   /// This is expected to be a good default optimization level for the vast
74:   /// majority of users.
75:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O2;
76:   /// Optimize for fast execution as much as possible.
77:   ///
78:   /// This mode is significantly more aggressive in trading off compile time
79:   /// and code size to get execution time improvements. The core idea is that
80:   /// this mode should include any optimization that helps execution time on
81:   /// balance across a diverse collection of benchmarks, even if it increases
82:   /// code size or compile time for some benchmarks without corresponding
83:   /// improvements to execution time.
84:   ///
85:   /// Despite being willing to trade more compile time off to get improved
86:   /// execution time, this mode still tries to avoid superlinear growth in
87:   /// order to make even significantly slower compile times at least scale
88:   /// reasonably. This does not preclude very substantial constant factor
89:   /// costs though.
90:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel O3;
91:   /// Similar to \c O2 but tries to optimize for small code size instead of
92:   /// fast execution without triggering significant incremental execution
93:   /// time slowdowns.
94:   ///
95:   /// The logic here is exactly the same as \c O2, but with code size and
96:   /// execution time metrics swapped.
97:   ///
98:   /// A consequence of the different core goal is that this should in general
99:   /// produce substantially smaller executables that still run in
100:   /// a reasonable amount of time.
101:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel Os;
102:   /// A very specialized mode that will optimize for code size at any and all
103:   /// costs.
104:   ///
105:   /// This is useful primarily when there are absolute size limitations and
106:   /// any effort taken to reduce the size is worth it regardless of the
107:   /// execution time impact. You should expect this level to produce rather
108:   /// slow, but very small, code.
109:   LLVM_ABI static const OptimizationLevel Oz;
110: 
111:   bool isOptimizingForSpeed() const { return SizeLevel == 0 && SpeedLevel > 0; }
112: 
113:   bool isOptimizingForSize() const { return SizeLevel > 0; }
114: 
115:   bool operator==(const OptimizationLevel &Other) const {
116:     return SizeLevel == Other.SizeLevel && SpeedLevel == Other.SpeedLevel;
117:   }
118:   bool operator!=(const OptimizationLevel &Other) const {
119:     return SizeLevel != Other.SizeLevel || SpeedLevel != Other.SpeedLevel;
120:   }
121: 
122:   unsigned getSpeedupLevel() const { return SpeedLevel; }

124:   unsigned getSizeLevel() const { return SizeLevel; }
125: };
126: } // namespace llvm

{"meta": null, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "{"content":"1: //===- CGSCCPassManager.h - Call graph pass management ----------*- C++ -*-===//\n2: //\n3: // Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.\n4: // See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.\n5: // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception\n6: //\n7: //===----------------------------------------------------------------------===//\n8: /// \\file\n9: ///\n10: /// This header provides classes for managing passes over SCCs of the call\n11: /// graph. These passes form an important component of LLVM's interprocedural\n12: /// optimizations. Because they operate on the SCCs of the call graph, and they\n13: /// traverse the graph in post-order, they can effectively do pair-wise\n14: /// interprocedural optimizations for all call edges in the program while\n15: /// incrementally refining it and improving the context of these pair-wise\n16: /// optimizations. At each call site edge, the callee has already been\n17: /// optimized as much as is possible. This in turn allows very accurate\n18: /// analysis of it for IPO.\n19: ///\n20: /// A secondary more general goal is to be able to isolate optimization on\n21: /// unrelated parts of the IR module. This is useful to ensure our\n22: /// optimizations are principled and don't miss oportunities where refinement\n23: /// of one part of the module influences transformations in another part of the\n24: /// module. But this is also useful if we want to parallelize the optimizations\n25: /// across common large module graph shapes which tend to be very wide and have\n26: /// large regions of unrelated cliques.\n27: ///\n28: /// To satisfy these goals, we use the LazyCallGraph which provides two graphs\n29: /// nested inside each other (and built lazily from the bottom-up): the call\n30: /// graph proper, and a reference graph. The reference graph is super set of\n31: /// the call graph and is a conservative approximation of what could through\n32: /// scalar or CGSCC transforms *become* the call graph. Using this allows us to\n33: /// ensure we optimize functions prior to them being introduced into the call\n34: /// graph by devirtualization or other technique, and thus ensures that\n35: /// subsequent pair-wise interprocedural optimizations observe the optimized\n36: /// form of these functions. The (potentially transitive) reference\n37: /// reachability used by the reference graph is a conservative approximation\n38: /// that still allows us to have independent regions of the graph.\n39: ///\n40: /// FIXME: There is one major drawback of the reference graph: in its naive\n41: /// form it is quadratic because it contains a distinct edge for each\n42: /// (potentially indirect) reference, even if are all through some common\n43: /// global table of function pointers. This can be fixed in a number of ways\n44: /// that essentially preserve enough of the normalization. While it isn't\n45: /// expected to completely preclude the usability of this, it will need to be\n46: /// addressed.\n47: ///\n48: ///\n49: /// All of these issues are made substantially more complex in the face of\n50: /// mutations to the call graph while optimization passes are being run. When\n51: /// mutations to the call graph occur we want to achieve two different things:\n52: ///\n53: /// - We need to update the call graph in-flight and invalidate analyses\n54: ///   cached on entities in the graph. Because of the cache-based analysis\n55: ///   design of the pass manager, it is essential to have stable identities for\n56: ///   the elements of the IR that passes traverse, and to invalidate any\n57: ///   analyses cached on these elements as the mutations take place.\n58: ///\n59: /// - We want to preserve the incremental and post-order traversal of the\n60: ///   graph even as it is refined and mutated. This means we want optimization\n61: ///   to observe the most refined form of the call graph and to do so in\n62: ///   post-order.\n63: ///\n64: /// To address this, the CGSCC manager uses both worklists that can be expanded\n65: /// by passes which transform the IR, and provides invalidation tests to skip\n66: /// entries that become dead. This extra data is provided to every SCC pass so\n67: /// that it can carefully update the manager's traversal as the call graph\n68: /// mutates.\n69: ///\n70: /// We also provide support for running function passes within the CGSCC walk,\n71: /// and there we provide automatic update of the call graph including of the\n72: /// pass manager to reflect call graph changes that fall out naturally as part\n73: /// of scalar transformations.\n74: ///\n75: /// The patterns used to ensure the goals of post-order visitation of the fully\n76: /// refined graph:\n77: ///\n78: /// 1) Sink toward the \"bottom\" as the graph is refined. These objects are set up by the RegisterPass\u003c\u003e\n27: /// template.\n28: ///\n29: class PassInfo {\n30: public:\n31:   using NormalCtor_t = Pass* (*)();\n32: \n33: private:\n34:   StringRef PassName;     // Nice name for Pass\n35:   StringRef PassArgument; // Command Line argument to run this pass\n36:   const void *PassID;\n37:   const bool IsCFGOnlyPass = false;      // Pass only looks at the CFG.\n38:   const bool IsAnalysis;                 // True if an analysis pass.\n39:   NormalCtor_t NormalCtor = nullptr;\n40: \n41: public:\n42:   /// PassInfo ctor - Do not call this directly, this should only be invoked\n43:   /// through RegisterPass.\n44:   PassInfo(StringRef name, StringRef arg, const void *pi, NormalCtor_t normal,\n45:            bool isCFGOnly, bool is_analysis)\n46:       : PassName(name), PassArgument(arg), PassID(pi), IsCFGOnlyPass(isCFGOnly),\n47:         IsAnalysis(is_analysis), NormalCtor(normal) {}\n48: \n49:   PassInfo(const PassInfo \u0026) = delete;\n50:   PassInfo \u0026operator=(const PassInfo \u0026) = delete;\n51: \n52:   /// getPassName - Return the friendly name for the pass, never returns null\n53:   StringRef getPassName() const { return PassName; }\n54: \n55:   /// getPassArgument - Return the command line option that may be passed to\n56:   /// 'opt' that will cause this pass to be run.  This will return null if there\n57:   /// is no argument.\n58:   StringRef getPassArgument() const { return PassArgument; }\n59: \n60:   /// getTypeInfo - Return the id object for the pass...\n61:   /// TODO : Rename\n62:   const void *getTypeInfo() const { return PassID; }\n63: \n64:   /// Return true if this PassID implements the specified ID pointer.\n65:   bool isPassID(const void *IDPtr) const { return PassID == IDPtr; }\n66: \n67:   bool isAnalysis() const { return IsAnalysis; }\n68: \n69:   /// isCFGOnlyPass - return true if this pass only looks at the CFG for the\n70:   /// function.\n71:   bool isCFGOnlyPass() const { return IsCFGOnlyPass; }\n72: \n73:   /// getNormalCtor - Return a pointer to a function, that when called, creates\n74:   /// an instance of the pass and returns it.  This pointer may be null if there\n75:   /// is no default constructor for the pass.\n76:   NormalCtor_t getNormalCtor() const {\n77:     return NormalCtor;\n78:   }\n79:   void setNormalCtor(NormalCtor_t Ctor) {\n80:     NormalCtor = Ctor;\n81:   }\n82: \n83:   /// createPass() - Use this method to create an instance of this pass.\n84:   Pass *createPass() const {\n85:     assert(NormalCtor \u0026\u0026\n86:            \"Cannot call createPass on PassInfo without default ctor!\");\n87:     return NormalCtor();\n88:   }\n89: };\n90: \n91: } // end namespace llvm\n92: \n93: #endif // LLVM_PASSINFO_H\n94: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/llvm-project--a8f3c97d@4152de6435984e9fc55431f4d74a7470d8ac05c0/-/blob/llvm/include/llvm/PassInfo.h"}", "annotations": null, "meta": null}], "structuredContent": {"content": "1: //===- llvm/PassInfo.h - Pass Info class ------------------------*- C++ -*-===//
2: //
3: // Part of the LLVM Project, under the Apache License v2.0 with LLVM Exceptions.
4: // See https://llvm.org/LICENSE.txt for license information.
5: // SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception
6: //

13: #ifndef LLVM_PASSINFO_H
14: #define LLVM_PASSINFO_H
15: 
16: #include "llvm/ADT/StringRef.h"
17: #include <cassert>
18: 
19: namespace llvm {
20: 
21: class Pass;
22: 
23: //===---------------------------------------------------------------------------
24: /// PassInfo class - An instance of this class exists for every pass known by
25: /// the system, and can be obtained from a live Pass by calling its
26: /// getPassInfo() method.  These objects are set up by the RegisterPass<>
27: /// template.
28: ///
29: class PassInfo {
30: public:
31:   using NormalCtor_t = Pass* (*)();
32: 
33: private:
34:   StringRef PassName;     // Nice name for Pass
35:   StringRef PassArgument; // Command Line argument to run this pass
36:   const void *PassID;
37:   const bool IsCFGOnlyPass = false;      // Pass only looks at the CFG.
38:   const bool IsAnalysis;                 // True if an analysis pass.
39:   NormalCtor_t NormalCtor = nullptr;
40: 
41: public:
42:   /// PassInfo ctor - Do not call this directly, this should only be invoked
43:   /// through RegisterPass.
44:   PassInfo(StringRef name, StringRef arg, const void *pi, NormalCtor_t normal,
45:            bool isCFGOnly, bool is_analysis)
46:       : PassName(name), PassArgument(arg), PassID(pi), IsCFGOnlyPass(isCFGOnly),
47:         IsAnalysis(is_analysis), NormalCtor(normal) {}
48: 
49:   PassInfo(const PassInfo &) = delete;
50:   PassInfo &operator=(const PassInfo &) = delete;
51: 
52:   /// getPassName - Return the friendly name for the pass, never returns null
53:   StringRef getPassName() const { return PassName; }
54: 
55:   /// getPassArgument - Return the command line option that may be passed to
56:   /// 'opt' that will cause this pass to be run.  This will return null if there
57:   /// is no argument.
58:   StringRef getPassArgument() const { return PassArgument; }
59: 
60:   /// getTypeInfo - Return the id object for the pass...
61:   /// TODO : Rename
62:   const void *getTypeInfo() const { return PassID; }
63: 
64:   /// Return true if this PassID implements the specified ID pointer.
65:   bool isPassID(const void *IDPtr) const { return PassID == IDPtr; }
66: 
67:   bool isAnalysis() const { return IsAnalysis; }
68: 
69:   /// isCFGOnlyPass - return true if this pass only looks at the CFG for the
70:   /// function.
71:   bool isCFGOnlyPass() const { return IsCFGOnlyPass; }
72: 
73:   /// getNormalCtor - Return a pointer to a function, that when called, creates
74:   /// an instance of the pass and returns it.  This pointer may be null if there
75:   /// is no default constructor for the pass.
76:   NormalCtor_t getNormalCtor() const {
77:     return NormalCtor;
78:   }
79:   void setNormalCtor(NormalCtor_t Ctor) {
80:     NormalCtor = Ctor;
81:   }
82: 
83:   /// createPass() - Use this method to create an instance of this pass.
84:   Pass *createPass() const {
85:     assert(NormalCtor &&
86:            "Cannot call createPass on PassInfo without default ctor!");
87:     return NormalCtor();
88:   }
89: };
90: 
91: } // end namespace llvm
92: 
93: #endif // LLVM_PASSINFO_H

- `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerImpl.h` — PassManager::run() implementation, AnalysisManager::getResultImpl(), invalidate()
- `llvm/lib/IR/PassManager.cpp` — Explicit template instantiations for PassManager<Module>, PassManager<Function>, AnalysisManager<Module>, AnalysisManager<Function>
- `llvm/lib/IR/PassRegistry.cpp` — PassRegistry::getPassRegistry(), PassRegistry::registerPass()

- `llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp` — buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline() implementation (O2/O3 pipeline: Annotation2Metadata → ForceFunctionAttrs → buildModuleSimplificationPipeline → buildModuleOptimizationPipeline)
- `llvm/lib/Passes/PassRegistry.def` — X-macro registry of all passes (MODULE_PASS, FUNCTION_PASS, MODULE_PASS_WITH_PARAMS)
- `llvm/include/llvm/Passes/TargetPassRegistry.inc` — Target-specific pass registration via AM.registerPass()
- `llvm/include/llvm/Passes/MachinePassRegistry.def` — Machine pass registry

- `llvm/include/llvm/Passes/CodeGenPassBuilder.h` — CodeGen pass builder

      "2. User calls PassBuilder::registerModuleAnalyses(MAM) which expands PassRegistry.def to call MAM.registerPass([&]{ return SomeAnalysis(); }) for each MODULE_ANALYSIS entry, storing AnalysisPassModel objects in AnalysisManager::AnalysisPasses map keyed by SomeAnalysis::ID().",
      "3. User calls MPM.run(M, MAM). PassManager<Module>::run() (in PassManagerImpl.h) iterates Passes in order.",
      "4. For each pass P: fetches PassInstrumentation PI = MAM.getResult<PassInstrumentationAnalysis>(M). Calls PI.runBeforePass(*P, M). Calls PreservedAnalyses PA = P->run(M, MAM) (virtual dispatch through PassModel::run -> SomePass::run).",
      "5. Inside SomePass::run(), if it needs an analysis it calls MAM.getResult<SomeAnalysis>(M). AnalysisManager::getResult() calls getResultImpl() which looks up the AnalysisPassModel in AnalysisPasses, calls its run() to compute the result (if not cached), stores it in AnalysisResults map, and returns a reference.",
      "6. After P->run() returns, PassManager calls PI.runAfterPass(*P, M, PA), then MAM.invalidate(M, PA) which walks all cached analysis results and calls result.invalidate() for any analysis not preserved by PA.",
      "7. Repeat for next pass in Passes vector."
    ]
  },

  "key_files_summary": {
    "llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h": "PassManager<IRUnitT> template (addPass, Passes vector), AnalysisManager<IRUnitT> template (registerPass, getResult, AnalysisPasses map), PassInfoMixin, AnalysisInfoMixin, InnerAnalysisManagerProxy, OuterAnalysisManagerProxy. Type aliases: ModulePassManager, FunctionPassManager, ModuleAnalysisManager, FunctionAnalysisManager.",
    "llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerInternal.h": "PassConcept<IRUnitT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...> (abstract virtual run()), PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, ...> (concrete wrapper), AnalysisPassConcept, AnalysisPassModel, AnalysisResultConcept, AnalysisResultModel.",
    "llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerImpl.h": "Out-of-line implementations of PassManager<IRUnitT>::run() and AnalysisManager<IRUnitT>::getResultImpl() / invalidate(). Contains the main pass execution loop with PassInstrumentation hooks.",
    "llvm/lib/IR/PassManager.cpp": "Explicit template instantiations for PassManager<Module>, PassManager<Function>, AnalysisManager<Module>, AnalysisManager<Function>.",
    "llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h": "Legacy PassRegistry class declaration. Singleton with DenseMap and StringMap of PassInfo*.",
    "llvm/lib/IR/PassRegistry.cpp": "PassRegistry::getPassRegistry() (Meyer's singleton), registerPass() (write-locked insert + listener notification), getPassInfo() (read-locked lookup).",
    "llvm/include/llvm/PassInfo.h": "PassInfo: metadata record (name, arg, ID pointer, NormalCtor function pointer, flags).",
    "llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h": "INITIALIZE_PASS_BEGIN/END macros (generate initialize##passName##Pass() functions), RegisterPass<T> template (file-scope static registration), PassRegistrationListener.",
    "llvm/include/llvm/Passes/PassBuilder.h": "PassBuilder class declaration: buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(), buildModuleSimplificationPipeline(), buildModuleOptimizationPipeline(), registerModuleAnalyses(), registerFunctionAnalyses(), registerCGSCCAnalyses(), registerLoopAnalyses(), parsePassPipeline(), extension point callbacks (registerPipelineStartEPCallback, registerVectorizerStartEPCallback, etc.).",
    "llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilder.cpp": "registerModuleAnalyses/registerFunctionAnalyses/etc. implementations (X-macro expansion of PassRegistry.def), parsePassPipeline() implementation (textual pipeline parsing).",
    "llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp": "buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(), buildModuleSimplificationPipeline(), buildModuleOptimizationPipeline(), buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline(), buildInlinerPipeline(), addVectorPasses(), and all other pipeline construction functions.",
    "llvm/lib/Passes/PassRegistry.def": "X-macro table of all NPM passes and analyses (MODULE_PASS, FUNCTION_PASS, MODULE_ANALYSIS, FUNCTION_ANALYSIS, CGSCC_PASS, LOOP_PASS, etc.).",
    "llvm/include/llvm/Passes/OptimizationLevel.h": "OptimizationLevel class with static constants O0, O1, O2 (SpeedLevel=2), O3 (SpeedLevel=3), Os, Oz."
  }
}

steps.message:
Here is the complete, sourced answer covering all four requested topics.

---

## 1. `PassManager<IRUnitT>` Template

**File:** `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h`

The New Pass Manager (NPM) is built around two cooperating templates:

### `PassManager<IRUnitT>`
Holds a `std::vector<std::unique_ptr<PassConceptT>> Passes`. The key method:

```cpp
// PassManager<IRUnitT>::addPass() — PassManager.h
template <typename PassT>
void addPass(PassT &&Pass) {
  using PassModelT = detail::PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, AnalysisManagerT, ExtraArgTs...>;
  Passes.emplace_back(new PassModelT(std::forward<PassT>(Pass)));
}
```

`PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, ...>` (defined in `llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManagerInternal.h`) stores the concrete pass **by value** and implements the abstract `PassConcept` interface via a single virtual `run()`. This is **concept-based polymorphism** — user pass classes need no virtual base class.

### `AnalysisManager<IRUnitT>`
Stores analyses in:
- `DenseMap<AnalysisKey*, unique_ptr<PassConceptT>> AnalysisPasses` — registered analyses
- `AnalysisResultListMapT AnalysisResultLists` + `AnalysisResultMapT AnalysisResults` — cached results

Type aliases used everywhere:
```cpp
using ModulePassManager    = PassManager<Module>;
using FunctionPassManager  = PassManager<Function>;
using ModuleAnalysisManager   = AnalysisManager<Module>;
using FunctionAnalysisManager = AnalysisManager<Function>;
```
Explicit instantiations live in `llvm/lib/IR/PassManager.cpp`.

---

## 2. `PassRegistry` — Two Distinct Systems

### 2a. Legacy PassRegistry (`llvm/include/llvm/PassRegistry.h` + `llvm/lib/IR/PassRegistry.cpp`)

The **legacy** (pre-NPM) registry is a process-global singleton:

```cpp
// PassRegistry.cpp
PassRegistry *PassRegistry::getPassRegistry() {
  static PassRegistry PassRegistryObj;   // Meyer's singleton
  return &PassRegistryObj;
}

void PassRegistry::registerPass(const PassInfo &PI, bool ShouldFree) {
  sys::SmartScopedWriter<true> Guard(Lock);
  PassInfoMap.insert({PI.getTypeInfo(), &PI});      // keyed by &PassClass::ID
  PassInfoStringMap[PI.getPassArgument()] = &PI;    // keyed by CLI arg string
  for (auto *L : Listeners) L->passRegistered(&PI); // notify listeners
}
```

`PassInfo` (`llvm/include/llvm/PassInfo.h`) is the metadata record: human name, CLI arg, `const void* PassID` (= `&PassClass::ID`), `NormalCtor_t` function pointer.

**Registration macros** (`llvm/include/llvm/PassSupport.h`):

```cpp
// INITIALIZE_PASS(passName, arg, name, cfg, analysis) expands to:
static void initializeMyPassPassOnce(PassRegistry &Registry) {
  PassInfo *PI = new PassInfo(name, arg, &MyPass::ID,
                              callDefaultCtor<MyPass>, cfg, analysis);
  Registry.registerPass(*PI, true);
}
static llvm::once_flag InitializeMyPassPassFlag;
void llvm::initializeMyPassPass(PassRegistry &Registry) {
  llvm::call_once(InitializeMyPassPassFlag, initializeMyPassPassOnce, Registry);
}

// Or at file scope for opt-visible passes:
static RegisterPass<MyPass> X("mypass", "My Pass Name");
// RegisterPass<T> ctor calls: PassRegistry::getPassRegistry()->registerPass(*this)
```

### 2b. NPM Analysis Registration (`llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h` lines 471–485)

```cpp
template <typename PassBuilderT>
bool registerPass(PassBuilderT &&PassBuilder) {
  using PassT = decltype(PassBuilder());
  using PassModelT = detail::AnalysisPassModel<IRUnitT, PassT, Invalidator, ExtraArgTs...>;
  auto &PassPtr = AnalysisPasses[PassT::ID()];
  if (PassPtr) return false;  // idempotent
  PassPtr.reset(new PassModelT(PassBuilder()));
  return true;
}
```

Called as: `MAM.registerPass([&] { return CallGraphAnalysis(); });`

---

## 3. `addPass()` Registration in the NPM

**File:** `llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilder.cpp` (lines 649–706) + `llvm/lib/Passes/PassRegistry.def`

`PassRegistry.def` is an **X-macro table** with no include guard, designed to be `#include`d multiple times:

```cpp
// PassRegistry.def (excerpt)
MODULE_ANALYSIS("callgraph",      CallGraphAnalysis())
MODULE_ANALYSIS("profile-summary", ProfileSummaryAnalysis())
MODULE_PASS("annotation2metadata", Annotation2MetadataPass())
MODULE_PASS("always-inline",       AlwaysInlinerPass())
FUNCTION_PASS("instcombine",       InstCombinePass())
// ... hundreds more entries
```

`PassBuilder::registerModuleAnalyses()` expands this table:

```cpp
void PassBuilder::registerModuleAnalyses(ModuleAnalysisManager &MAM) {
#define MODULE_ANALYSIS(NAME, CREATE_PASS)          \
  MAM.registerPass([&] { return CREATE_PASS; });
#include "PassRegistry.def"
  for (auto &C : ModuleAnalysisRegistrationCallbacks) C(MAM); // plugin hooks
}

void PassBuilder::registerFunctionAnalyses(FunctionAnalysisManager &FAM) {
  FAM.registerPass([&] { return buildDefaultAAPipeline(); }); // AA first
#define FUNCTION_ANALYSIS(NAME, CREATE_PASS)                              \
  if constexpr (std::is_constructible_v<                                  \
                    std::remove_reference_t<decltype(CREATE_PASS)>,       \
                    const TargetMachine &>) {                             \
    if (TM) FAM.registerPass([&] { return CREATE_PASS; });               \
  } else { FAM.registerPass([&] { return CREATE_PASS; }); }
#include "PassRegistry.def"
  for (auto &C : FunctionAnalysisRegistrationCallbacks) C(FAM);
}
```

The same `.def` file is also used by `parsePassPipeline()` to map textual pass names to `addPass()` calls.

---

## 4. O2/O3 Standard Optimization Pipeline Execution Order

**Entry point:** `PassBuilder::buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(OptimizationLevel Level, ThinOrFullLTOPhase Phase)`  
**File:** `llvm/lib/Passes/PassBuilderPipelines.cpp` (line 1715)

`OptimizationLevel::O2` = `{SpeedLevel=2, SizeLevel=0}`, `O3` = `{SpeedLevel=3, SizeLevel=0}` (`llvm/include/llvm/Passes/OptimizationLevel.h`).

### Complete Pipeline Order

```
buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline(O2/O3)
│
├─ [non-LTO] MemProfRemoveInfo                          // strip MemProf metadata
├─ Annotation2MetadataPass                              // @llvm.global.annotations → !annotation
├─ ForceFunctionAttrsPass                               // force pipeline-required attrs
├─ [PGO debug] AddDiscriminatorsPass (via FPM adaptor)
├─ invokePipelineStartEPCallbacks                       // user extension point
│
├─ buildModuleSimplificationPipeline()  ─────────────────────────────────────────
│   ├─ [PseudoProbe] SampleProfileProbePass
│   ├─ [ThinLTO post-link] PGOIndirectCallPromotion
│   ├─ InferFunctionAttrsPass
│   ├─ CoroEarlyPass
│   ├─ EarlyFPM (via module→function adaptor):
│   │   ├─ EntryExitInstrumenterPass
│   │   ├─ LowerExpectIntrinsicPass
│   │   ├─ SimplifyCFGPass
│   │   ├─ SROAPass(ModifyCFG)
│   │   ├─ EarlyCSEPass
│   │   └─ [O3 only] CallSiteSplittingPass  ◄── O3 addition
│   ├─ [SamplePGO] SampleProfileLoaderPass + RequireAnalysisPass<ProfileSummaryAnalysis>
│   ├─ OpenMPOptPass
│   ├─ AttributorPass / AttributorLightPass
│   ├─ invokePipelineEarlySimplificationEPCallbacks
│   ├─ IPSCCPPass
│   ├─ CalledValuePropagationPass
│   ├─ GlobalOptPass
│   ├─ GlobalCleanupPM: PromotePass → InstCombinePass → SimplifyCFGPass
│   ├─ [PGO] PGO instrumentation/use passes
│   └─ buildInlinerPipeline() [CGSCC inliner wrapper]:
│       ├─ RequireAnalysisPass<GlobalsAA> + InvalidateAnalysisPass<AAManager>
│       ├─ RequireAnalysisPass<ProfileSummaryAnalysis>
│       └─ CGSCCPassManager (MainCGPipeline):
│           ├─ AttributorCGSCCPass / AttributorLightCGSCCPass
│           ├─ PostOrderFunctionAttrsPass(SkipNonRecursive=true)
│           ├─ [O3 only] ArgumentPromotionPass  ◄── O3 addition
│           ├─ [O2/O3] OpenMPOptCGSCCPass  ◄── O2/O3 addition
│           ├─ invokeCGSCCOptimizerLateEPCallbacks
│           ├─ buildFunctionSimplificationPipeline() (via CGSCC→FPM adaptor)
│           ├─ PostOrderFunctionAttrsPass
│           ├─ CoroSplitPass + CoroAnnotationElidePass
│           └─ [late module] InvalidateAnalysisPass<ShouldNotRunFunctionPassesAnalysis>
│
└─ buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()  ───────────────────────────────────────────
    ├─ [if enabled] PartialInlinerPass
    ├─ [non-LTO-prelink] EliminateAvailableExternallyPass
    ├─ ReversePostOrderFunctionAttrsPass
    ├─ [CS-PGO] post-inline PGO instrumentation/use
    ├─ [global analyses] RecomputeGlobalsAAPass
    ├─ invokeOptimizerEarlyEPCallbacks
    ├─ OptimizePM (FunctionPassManager, via module→function adaptor):
    │   ├─ DropUnnecessaryAssumesPass
    │   ├─ [if enabled] LoopVersioningLICMPass + LICMPass
    │   ├─ Float2IntPass
    │   ├─ LowerConstantIntrinsicsPass
    │   ├─ [matrix] LowerMatrixIntrinsicsPass + EarlyCSEPass
    │   ├─ [O3+CHR] ControlHeightReductionPass  ◄── O3 addition
    │   ├─ invokeVectorizerStartEPCallbacks
    │   ├─ LPM: LoopRotatePass → LoopDeletionPass → [if enabled] LoopInterchangePass
    │   ├─ [if enabled] LoopFusePass
    │   ├─ LoopDistributePass
    │   ├─ InjectTLIMappings
    │   ├─ addVectorPasses():
    │   │   ├─ LoopVectorizePass
    │   │   ├─ [SLP] SLPVectorizerPass + EarlyCSEPass
    │   │   ├─ VectorCombinePass
    │   │   ├─ SimplifyCFGPass (aggressive options)
    │   │   ├─ [FullLTO] SCCPPass + InstCombinePass + BDCEPass
    │   │   ├─ InstCombinePass
    │   │   ├─ [if enabled] LoopUnrollAndJamPass
    │   │   ├─ LoopUnrollPass
    │   │   ├─ WarnMissedTransformationsPass
    │   │   ├─ SROAPass(PreserveCFG)
    │   │   ├─ InferAlignmentPass
    │   │   ├─ InstCombinePass
    │   │   ├─ LICMPass (with MemorySSA)
    │   │   └─ AlignmentFromAssumptionsPass
    │   ├─ invokeVectorizerEndEPCallbacks
    │   ├─ LoopSinkPass
    │   ├─ InstSimplifyPass
    │   ├─ DivRemPairsPass
    │   ├─ TailCallElimPass
    │   └─ SimplifyCFGPass (final CFG cleanup)
    ├─ [non-LTO-prelink] AllocTokenPass
    ├─ invokeOptimizerLastEPCallbacks
    ├─ [if enabled] HotColdSplittingPass
    ├─ [if enabled] IROutlinerPass
    ├─ GlobalDCEPass
    ├─ ConstantMergePass
    ├─ [if enabled] MergeFunctionsPass
    ├─ [if enabled] CGProfilePass
    ├─ [non-LTO-prelink] RelLookupTableConverterPass
    └─ [speculative devirt] WholeProgramDevirtPass + LowerTypeTestsPass + re-inliner
```

### End-to-End Execution Flow

1. **Registration:** `PassBuilder::buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline()` calls `MPM.addPass(SomePass())` for each pass. `addPass()` wraps it in `PassModel<Module, SomePass, ...>` and appends to `Passes` vector.

2. **Analysis registration:** `PassBuilder::registerModuleAnalyses(MAM)` expands `PassRegistry.def` to call `MAM.registerPass([&]{ return SomeAnalysis(); })` for each `MODULE_ANALYSIS` entry, storing `AnalysisPassModel` objects keyed by `SomeAnalysis::ID()`.

3. **Execution:** `MPM.run(M, MAM)` (in `PassManagerImpl.h`) iterates `Passes` in insertion order. For each pass `P`:
   - Fetches `PassInstrumentation PI` from `MAM.getResult<PassInstrumentationAnalysis>(M)`
   - Calls `PI.runBeforePass(*P, M)`
   - Calls `PreservedAnalyses PA = P->run(M, MAM)` — one virtual dispatch through `PassModel::run` → `SomePass::run()`
   - Calls `PI.runAfterPass(*P, M, PA)`
   - Calls `MAM.invalidate(M, PA)` — walks cached results, calls `result.invalidate()` for anything not in `PA`

4. **On-demand analysis:** Inside `SomePass::run()`, `MAM.getResult<SomeAnalysis>(M)` triggers `getResultImpl()` which finds the `AnalysisPassModel`, calls its `run()` to compute (if not cached), stores in `AnalysisResults`, and returns a reference.

---

arguments.message:
The task is complete. All four requested topics have been fully traced and documented:

1. **PassManager template** (`llvm/include/llvm/IR/PassManager.h`): `PassManager<IRUnitT>` uses concept-based polymorphism — `addPass()` wraps each concrete pass in a `PassModel<IRUnitT, PassT>` (from `PassManagerInternal.h`) that implements the abstract `PassConcept` virtual interface, then appends it to a `std::vector<unique_ptr<PassConceptT>> Passes`. `PassManager::run()` (in `PassManagerImpl.h`) iterates this vector in insertion order.

2. **PassRegistry** — two systems:
   - **Legacy** (`PassRegistry.h`/`PassRegistry.cpp`): Process-global Meyer's singleton with `DenseMap<const void*, PassInfo*>` and `StringMap<PassInfo*>`. Registration via `INITIALIZE_PASS` macros or `RegisterPass<T>` file-scope statics (both in `PassSupport.h`).
   - **NPM** (`AnalysisManager::registerPass()` in `PassManager.h`): Takes a lambda, deduces `PassT`, stores an `AnalysisPassModel` in `DenseMap<AnalysisKey*, unique_ptr<PassConceptT>> AnalysisPasses`.

3. **pass add() registration** (`PassBuilder.cpp` + `PassRegistry.def`): `PassRegistry.def` is an X-macro table. `PassBuilder::registerModuleAnalyses()` etc. expand it to call `AM.registerPass([&]{ return CREATE_PASS; })` for every entry. `parsePassPipeline()` uses the same table to map textual names to `addPass()` calls.

4. **O2/O3 pipeline execution order** (`PassBuilderPipelines.cpp`): `buildPerModuleDefaultPipeline()` → `Annotation2MetadataPass` → `ForceFunctionAttrsPass` → `buildModuleSimplificationPipeline()` (early FPM cleanup, OpenMP, Attributor, IPSCCP, GlobalOpt, CGSCC inliner with ArgumentPromotion@O3, function simplification) → `buildModuleOptimizationPipeline()` (RPO attrs, GlobalsAA recompute, OptimizePM with loop rotation/vectorization/unrolling/SLP, GlobalDCE, ConstantMerge).

The full structured answer is saved at `/workspace/answer.json`.
